description:
The Music Mission team owns Spotify’s end to end proposition for music creators and the experiences they create for fans. The team is dedicated to building tools and services to enable creation, promotion, expression, and monetization at scale.

You’ll join Spotify’s Editorial team in India, where music, culture, and storytelling come together to shape how millions of listeners discover and connect with music every day. As part of a global network of editors, you’ll help define how international music and hip-hop show up for audiences in India—and how Indian hip-hop travels beyond borders.

lists.content:
• Spot and act on music trends, emerging artists, and cultural moments across international and hip-hop scenes
  • Curate and evolve playlists that connect with listeners, using thoughtful sequencing, strong storytelling, and clear editorial voice
  • Translate global music trends into locally relevant experiences for Indian audiences, while elevating Indian hip-hop to global listeners
  • Use data and audience insights to continuously improve playlist performance and listener engagement
  • Collaborate with global and regional editorial teams on programming strategies and campaigns
  • Partner with marketing, advertising, and product teams to bring cultural insights into broader Spotify initiatives
  • Contribute to artist discovery by identifying breakout talent and supporting their growth through editorial ecosystems
  • Create engaging editorial content beyond playlists, including written and multimedia storytelling

• You have at least 6+ years of experience in music, media, or entertainment, with a strong understanding of digital platforms
  • You have deep knowledge of global music culture, especially hip-hop and international genres, and how they connect with audiences in India
  • You understand how listener behavior, culture, and trends intersect, and can translate that into meaningful editorial decisions
  • You’re comfortable working with data and using insights to guide your thinking and measure impact
  • You communicate clearly and confidently in English; additional Indian languages are a plus
  • You bring a strong point of view on music and culture, paired with curiosity and openness to new perspectives
  • You collaborate effectively across teams and are comfortable working in a fast-paced, evolving environment
  • You’re organized, adaptable, and bring a thoughtful, inclusive approach to your work

• This role is based in Mumbai
  • We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! There will be some in person meetings, but still allows for flexibility to work from home.

    •

descriptionPlain:
At Spotify, our vision is to provide the perfect recommendations for every moment. We've already connected millions of people to their favorite songs and podcasts, and created a service that people use to discover and share the content they love.
We are now growing our world-class Audiobook editorial team, looking for individuals who can connect amazing stories with the just right moods, moments and genres to listeners who will love them.
Spotify is seeking an Editor, Audiobooks – Southern Europe to join our Audiobooks team. In this role, you will build and develop editorial programming across key Southern European markets, including Italy, Spain, and Portugal, with a focus on non-English language storefronts. You will grow your experience of strategic collaboration with cross-functional teams to support key marketing campaigns and celebrate unique cultural moments.
The right candidate has a solid grounding in books editorial and/or bookselling, has gained hands-on experience with digital content, and is developing a deep understanding of the publishing industry with a genuine passion for all things books. You'll be fluent in Italian and Spanish and have a deep knowledge of the cultures and publishing landscapes of your markets. Fluency in an additional Southern European language, such as Portuguese, would be a bonus. You'll be organised, a strong communicator, and eager to grow in a fast-paced, collaborative environment working alongside a variety of teams and partners.

You will grow your experience of strategic collaboration with cross-functional teams to support key marketing campaigns and celebrate unique cultural moments.

The right candidate has a solid grounding in books editorial and/or bookselling, has gained hands-on experience with digital content, and is developing a deep understanding of the publishing industry with a genuine passion for all things books. You'll be fluent in Italian and Spanish and have a deep knowledge of the cultures and publishing landscapes of your markets. Fluency in an additional Southern European language, such as Portuguese, would be a bonus. You'll be organised, a strong communicator, and eager to grow in a fast-paced, collaborative environment working alongside a variety of teams and partners.

lists.content:
• Develop and contribute to editorial strategy for audiobooks across Southern European markets, including Italy, Spain, and Portugal, building your understanding of regional publishing landscapes and cultural moments.

  • Manage ongoing editorial updates, collections, and playlists, with a focus on continuous improvement and testing of new approaches.

  • Collaborate on and implement editorial recommendations based on a growing understanding of data and business goals, to attract new and diverse audiences.

  • Develop day-to-day relationships with sales representatives at key publishing houses across the region, including Italian- and Spanish-language publishers.

  • Support the programming of editorial content on platform as needed.

  • Use research, cultural insights, and data analytics to continually enhance the editorial lens for our audiobooks platform.

  • Collaborate cross-functionally with wider Spotify teams — including growth, marketing, PR, product, engineering, and operations — to highlight and champion audiobooks.

  • Track, analyse, and communicate key quantitative metrics and business trends.

• 3–5 years of experience editing, curating, or merchandising content at a digital-first platform, a publishing house, or a major books retailer.

  • Fluency in Italian and Spanish and strong English communication skills, with the ability to collaborate with global teams; proficiency in Portuguese or other Southern European languages is a plus.

  • A growing understanding of the Southern European publishing landscape and a passion for connecting listeners with great content.

  • An ability to balance commercial recommendations with diverse, representative programming.

  • A keen sense of what makes titles appropriate for different situations, and an ability to quickly identify authors, series, and titles to recommend.

  • Comfort using both quantitative and qualitative data to inform editorial decisions.

  • Experience building and implementing diverse recommendations that reflect a broad range of cultures, people, and perspectives.

  • A passion for the evolution of audio and enthusiasm for spoken word content.

  • Strong organisational skills and excellent attention to detail.

This role is based in Madrid, Spain or London, UK

  • We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! There will be some in person meetings, but still allows for flexibility to work from home

  • We ask that you come in 2-3 times per week.

additionalPlain: The United States base range for this position is $164,448.00 - $234,926.00, plus equity. The benefits available for this position include health insurance, six month paid parental leave, 401(k) retirement plan, monthly meal allowance, 23 paid days off, 13 paid flexible holidays, paid sick leave. This range encompasses multiple levels. Leveling is determined during the interview process. Placement in a level depends on relevant work history and interview performance. These ranges may be modified in the future.

descriptionPlain:
We are looking for an Engineering Manager to drive Advertising engineering leadership and practices. In this role, you will be instrumental in growing the team and guiding development to successfully scale. You will help us to create a user-first ad experience that's personalized and relevant so we can grow to billions of fans, increasing engagement with our listeners and providing better value to our advertisers. Above all, your work will impact the way the world experiences music.
 
This squad is responsible for the foundational data, services, and tooling that power the ad-serving ecosystem, with a focus on three core areas: Data Architecture and Integrity, Targeting Data Propagation, and Observability and Debugging. They design and own the data architecture to ensure the accuracy and timeliness of core ad-serving datasets for pacing, billing, and business analysis. They also manage the services and pipelines that reliably deliver critical user-targeting information—such as privacy preferences, ad history, and audience segment data—to the ad-serving path. Furthermore, the team develops tools and mechanisms to enhance telemetry within the ad-serving stack, enabling more effective debugging and easier evaluation of system changes, while also providing necessary support for new feature development by handling their specific data and event functionality requirements.

lists.content:
• Build and lead a robust team of data and backend engineers by attracting top talent, mentoring individuals and managing conflict.
  • Work with product managers and lead the team to design and implement product features, while improving the quality of the current big-data intensive tools that exist for audiences and targeting.
  • Lead the team to utilize, homogenize and make available for peer teams to use, diverse large-volume datasets built around user preferences, behavior, identity and location - gathered from a user's mobile as well as other connected platforms.
  • Strong understanding of how data-intensive systems are expressed in the UX shown to customers.
  • Grow the technical expertise of the team around system design, quality and testing, scalability, performance and fault tolerance.
  • Manage OKRs, roadmaps, career conversations, performance and accountability, and thereby carefully plan, track, and report on work of the team and identify problems early.
  • Work closely with many peer teams to ensure that our systems are designed in a scalable and maintainable manner.
  • Nurture a culture of technical quality from design, through code review, to production.
  • Drive optimization, testing and tooling to improve data quality.

• Y​ou have experience in leading, managing, coaching and mentoring software developers
  • You have 6+ years Experience in object-oriented programming including Java, Python.
  • You have experience working with high volume heterogeneous data, with data tools such as Hadoop,
  • You have experience with BigTable, BigQuery and Hive.
  • You have experience in data modeling, data access and data storage techniques.
  • You have designed and built distributed production services / pipelines with data processing frameworks like Scio, Storm, Spark and the Google Cloud Platform.
  • You have led agile ceremonies including sprint planning, daily standups and retrospectives
  • You have experience with yearly and quarterly project roadmap planning including sizing, scoping, prioritizing, sequencing and defining external dependencies
  • You have mentored and coached software engineers

• We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! For this role, it can be within the North America region in which we have a work location
  • This team operates within the Eastern Standard time zone for collaboration

descriptionPlain:
The Platform team creates the technology that enables Spotify to learn quickly and scale easily, enabling rapid growth in our users and our business around the globe. Spanning many disciplines, we work to make the business work; creating the infrastructure, tooling, frameworks, and capabilities needed to welcome a billion customers.

What if the systems you built changed how millions of developers work — not just at Spotify, but across the industry? The Platform Developer Experience (PDX) team builds the infrastructure and internal products that power how Spotify engineers ship software. Within PDX, the Wingspan team is building the next generation of fleet management and reimagining how we interact with AI agents — and bringing those capabilities to market through Portal, Spotify’s external developer platform built on Backstage.
 
We are redesigning platform engineering around intelligent agents, large language models, and decision-aware automation. In this role, you’ll lead a team shaping how AI becomes a core operating layer of modern platform infrastructure, directly improving how developers ship and maintain software at scale.
description:
The Platform team creates the technology that enables Spotify to learn quickly and scale easily, enabling rapid growth in our users and our business around the globe. Spanning many disciplines, we work to make the business work; creating the infrastructure, tooling, frameworks, and capabilities needed to welcome a billion customers.

What if the systems you built changed how millions of developers work — not just at Spotify, but across the industry? The Platform Developer Experience (PDX) team builds the infrastructure and internal products that power how Spotify engineers ship software. Within PDX, the Wingspan team is building the next generation of fleet management and reimagining how we interact with AI agents — and bringing those capabilities to market through Portal, Spotify’s external developer platform built on Backstage.
 
We are redesigning platform engineering around intelligent agents, large language models, and decision-aware automation. In this role, you’ll lead a team shaping how AI becomes a core operating layer of modern platform infrastructure, directly improving how developers ship and maintain software at scale.

lists.content:
• Lead and grow a team building AI-native backend systems that orchestrate large-scale, autonomous fleet changes
• Define the technical vision for integrating LLM-powered reasoning and agent-based execution into production infrastructure
• Architect systems where AI agents analyze service metadata, generate safe change plans, execute updates, and validate outcomes
• Establish safety boundaries, evaluation frameworks, and observability models for AI-driven automation at scale
• Drive responsible AI adoption by embedding governance, auditability, and human-in-the-loop safeguards into system design
• Balance rapid experimentation with platform reliability, ensuring intelligent automation meets production-grade standards
• Partner with product and go-to-market teams to position Portal and AI fleet management as a leading developer platform
• Build a team culture grounded in curiosity, technical rigor, and continuous learning in emerging AI systems

• You have 3+ years of experience leading engineering teams working on distributed systems or developer platforms at scale
• You have 5+ years of experience in backend engineering with strong expertise in Java, Python, and/or Node.js
• You have experience integrating AI/ML systems, LLMs, agent frameworks, or AI-assisted tooling into real-world systems
• You understand how autonomous systems make decisions and how to design guardrails that ensure safety, reliability, and traceability
• You think in systems, considering data flows, model behavior, orchestration layers, and operational impact
• You are comfortable leading in emerging technical domains where patterns are still forming
• You create inclusive environments where diverse perspectives strengthen technical and product decisions
• You communicate clearly across engineers, product leaders, and executives about both the opportunities and risks of AI-driven systems

• We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! For this role, you can be within the EMEA region as long as we have a work location (excluding France due to on-call restrictions).
•This team operates within the Central European and GMT time zone for collaboration

additionalPlain:
Spotify is an equal opportunity employer. You are welcome at Spotify for who you are, no matter where you come from, what you look like, or what’s playing in your headphones. Our platform is for everyone, and so is our workplace. The more voices we have represented and amplified in our business, the more we will all thrive, contribute, and be forward-thinking! So bring us your personal experience, your perspectives, and your background. It’s in our differences that we will find the power to keep revolutionizing the way the world listens.
At Spotify, we are passionate about inclusivity and making sure our entire recruitment process is accessible to everyone. We have ways to request reasonable accommodations during the interview process and help assist in what you need. If you need accommodations at any stage of the application or interview process, please let us know - we’re here to support you in any way we can.
Spotify transformed music listening forever when we launched in 2008. Our mission is to unlock the potential of human creativity by giving a million creative artists the opportunity to live off their art and billions of fans the chance to enjoy and be passionate about these creators. Everything we do is driven by our love for music and podcasting. Today, we are the world’s most popular audio streaming subscription service.

descriptionPlain:
The Platform team creates the technology that enables Spotify to learn quickly and scale easily, enabling rapid growth in our users and our business around the globe. Spanning many disciplines, we work to make the business work; creating the infrastructure, tooling, frameworks, and capabilities needed to welcome a billion customers.
You’ll join the Learning Infrastructure Studio, a critical part of Spotify’s Platform mission. Our teams build the foundational infrastructure and intelligence layer that powers data science work at Spotify. We serve a rapidly expanding set of users, from specialist data scientists doing deep analytical work, to product builders, business analysts, and increasingly, AI agents that need the right context, skills, and guardrails to work effectively with data.

lists.content:
• Lead, grow, and support a team of engineers, fostering a culture of technical excellence, psychological safety, and continuous learning
  • Set technical direction and partner closely with senior engineers to evolve our learning platforms
  • Partner deeply with product managers and technical leads to shape the roadmap and ensure engineering perspective is present from strategy through to delivery.
  • Drive delivery of high-impact initiatives, balancing speed, quality, and long-term sustainability
  • Foster a strong engineering culture grounded in inclusion, ownership, and continuous improvement
  • Coach and mentor engineers, helping them develop their technical and leadership skills
  • Contribute to org-wide strategy for data science infrastructure and platform capabilities
  • Help the team navigate a fast-moving AI landscape: evaluate emerging tooling, integrate new capabilities thoughtfully, and build engineering practices that are ready for a world where humans and agents work together.
  • Stay close to the code and systems: participate in design reviews, guide architecture, and maintain enough hands-on context to make high-quality technical decisions.
  • Act as a technical multiplier, stepping in on ambiguous or high-stakes problems while ensuring the team remains the primary driver of delivery.

• You have experience managing and developing engineering teams in infrastructure, platform, or data and analytics tooling context.
  • You've led teams building infrastructure or platform capabilities used at scale. You understand what it means to be a foundational layer that others depend on.
  • You’re well-versed in how AI is shifting data and analytics workflows, and you bring informed opinions about how engineering teams should adapt and grow into that future. You’re doing AI-native development and can guide engineers to transform AI-native tools for data scientists.
  • You know how to balance technical depth with people leadership and stakeholder collaboration
  • You care about building inclusive, psychologically safe teams where everyone can do their best work
  • You have a track record of delivering complex technical projects with measurable impact
  • You are comfortable navigating ambiguity and helping teams prioritize effectively
  • You’re comfortable operating as a player–coach, balancing leadership with hands-on technical contribution, and you know how to scale yourself by contributing selectively rather than becoming a bottleneck.

• This role is based in Toronto
  • We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! There will be some in person meetings, but still allows for flexibility to work from home.

descriptionPlain:
Think it, build it, ship it, tweak it" is at the core of Spotify's product development approach. In the Experimentation product area we are providing Spotify and external customers with Confidence, our platform to quickly and safely iterate through ideas, learn from them and make informed decisions for better products.
 
At Spotify, experimentation touches almost all of our functions from marketing via content management to consumer products, and we’re running several thousands of tests every year. Through confidence, we 're sharing all our findings and practices with more companies as a commercial SaaS product.
 
The team you will manage is a skilled and highly engaged group of engineers that are distributed over Europe. As an Engineering manager, you coach them individually and as a team, maintaining their strong team culture and helping them thrive and and grow. Your technical experience provides mentorship and guidelines to the team, which ensures quality and timeliness of your deliveries. Your leadership promotes creativity, collaboration and courage, which forms a kind and productive team with a high bar for quality and pace of delivery.

lists.content:
• You will hire, coach and develop the careers of a team of engineers
  • You will be responsible for high-quality deliveries
  • You will partner with product managers to support with prioritizations and product visions
  • You will support the engineering team and work with Staff engineers to formulate the technical strategy needed to deliver on our vision
  • You will do hands on engineering work from time to time
  • In the end, your primary goal is to support a highly effective engineering team delivering an amazing product

• You thrive when developing great people, not just great products and cultivating a strong engineering culture in an agile environment
  • You are skilled in collaborating on and developing distributed systems that can scale to millions of users.
  • You have experience with data warehouses and data processing at scale
  • You have worked on commercial SaaS products in the past and have hands on experience taking features from zero to one
  • You are a brave learner and see unfamiliar territories as an opportunity to grow.
  • You are a senior individual contributor (or has been) with strong people skills and leadership experience
  • You’re passionate about software engineering best practices and love to have an impact on large-scale tech problems
  • You care deeply about quality of the products your working and, and hold people to a high standard
  • Naturally, you harness AI as a tool that helps you be more effective at your work

• Based in Stockholm, Sweden
  • We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! There will be some in person meetings, but still allows for flexibility to work from home. We ask that you come in 3 times per week.

descriptionPlain:
Delivering the best Spotify experience possible. To as many people as possible. In as many moments as possible. That’s what the Experience Mission is all about. We use our deep understanding of consumer expectations to enrich the lives of millions of users around the world, bringing the music, audio, and video they love to the devices, apps, and platforms they use every day.
The Content Platform team powers the full lifecycle of content across music, podcasts, audiobooks, and emerging formats. We make sure that everything—from licensed tracks to creator submissions and user-generated content—is delivered in a way that’s trusted, safe, and high quality at global scale. Our work sits at the heart of Spotify’s ecosystem, shaping how content is ingested, understood, governed, and distributed.
We’re seeking an Engineering Manager to lead a team focused on delivering content across Spotify’s ecosystem—ensuring that metadata and media flow efficiently and reliably from a wide range of internal systems to our apps and partner platforms. This work underpins every play, recommendation, and interaction, requiring systems that are scalable, observable, and resilient.

lists.content:
• Lead and grow a team of engineers building large-scale systems that power how content is delivered across Spotify

  • Set technical direction and guide the evolution of backend systems responsible for content distribution

  • Partner closely with product, design, and platform teams to deliver reliable, high-impact solutions

  • Ensure highly available, scalable, and performant infrastructure supporting millions of content interactions

  • Foster a culture of ownership, collaboration, and continuous improvement within your team

  • Support engineers through coaching, feedback, and career development

  • Drive execution across complex, cross-functional initiatives involving multiple systems and stakeholders

  • Champion best practices in reliability, observability, and system design

• You have experience leading and developing engineering teams in a fast-paced, product-driven environment

  • You bring strong technical experience in distributed systems, backend platforms, or infrastructure at scale

  • You know how to balance technical direction with people leadership, creating clarity and focus for your team

  • You are comfortable working across teams and aligning stakeholders toward shared goals

  • You care deeply about building reliable systems that impact millions of users

  • You have experience with high-throughput, low-latency systems or data/content delivery pipelines

  • You communicate clearly and foster an environment where ideas are shared openly

  • You are motivated by developing others and building inclusive, high-performing teams

• This role is based in Stockholm or London

  • We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! There will be some in-person meetings, but still allows for flexibility to work from home

descriptionPlain:
We design Spotify’s consumer experience—end to end, moment to moment, across every screen, platform, and partner integration. Our mission is to make listening feel effortless, personal, and joyful for billions of users around the world. That means turning complexity into clarity across hundreds of touchpoints—from our mobile and desktop apps to the smart speakers, TVs, cars, and integrations where Spotify shows up every day. If it touches a consumer, we shape it. We bring deep insight into human behavior, design, and technology to craft experiences that feel intuitive, expressive, and unmistakably Spotify.
You’ll join the Core Experience Studio within Spotify’s Experience Mission, working in the Format Foundations Product Area. This space builds the foundations behind how users experience artists, albums, podcasts, and more across the app.
Within this area, you’ll lead Meadow — a backend platform team that powers scalable, content-agnostic metadata delivery. Meadow sits at the heart of many Spotify experiences, enabling teams across Music, Podcasts, Audiobooks, and beyond to build consistent, high-quality user experiences.

lists.content:
• Lead a backend engineering team, with accountability for delivery, technical direction, and team health

  • Define and evolve the technical strategy for metadata delivery systems, including fan-out serving, versioned contracts, and caching

  • Shape the long-term vision of the team in line with Core Experience and broader platform strategy

  • Partner closely with product, design, and engineering leaders to align priorities and drive execution

  • Enable adoption of platform capabilities across multiple consumer-facing teams and content verticals

  • Guide architectural decisions, review implementations, and help unblock complex technical challenges

  • Drive adoption of AI-assisted development practices to improve speed, quality, and developer experience

  • Support and grow engineers, fostering an inclusive and collaborative team environment

• You have strong experience building and scaling backend systems for data delivery or platform infrastructure

  • You understand patterns like fan-out architectures, API versioning, and caching strategies

  • You have worked across end-to-end data systems, from pipelines to distribution layers

  • You have experience leading engineering teams and supporting individuals in their growth

  • You’ve contributed to cross-team technical strategy and aligned stakeholders around shared outcomes

  • You are comfortable working with AI-assisted development tools and guiding teams in their use

  • You think in systems and platforms, balancing the needs of internal developers and end users

  • You care deeply about building inclusive teams where people feel supported and empowered

  • You’re curious, adaptable, and comfortable navigating ambiguity

• This role is based in London or Stockholm

  • We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! There will be some in person meetings, but still allows for flexibility to work from home.

Spotify is an equal opportunity employer. You are welcome at Spotify for who you are, no matter where you come from, what you look like, or what’s playing in your headphones. descriptionPlain:
The Platform team creates the technology that enables Spotify to learn quickly and scale easily, enabling rapid growth in our users and our business around the globe. Spanning many disciplines, we work to make the business work; creating the infrastructure, tooling, frameworks, and capabilities needed to welcome a billion customers.
You’ll join the Core Infrastructure studio within Spotify’s Platform mission, specifically the Manifesto squad. This team builds and evolves the foundational infrastructure that powers Spotify at scale—helping hundreds of engineers ship faster, more reliably, and with confidence. You’ll lead a team working on critical systems that enable resilience, performance, and developer productivity across the company.

lists.content:
• Lead, support, and grow a team of engineers working on core infrastructure systems used across Spotify
  • Set clear direction and priorities, balancing long-term platform investments with immediate product needs
  • Partner closely with product, engineering, and cross-functional leaders to shape infrastructure strategy
  • Drive technical excellence, reliability, and scalability across distributed systems
  • Foster a healthy, inclusive, and collaborative team environment where people can do their best work
  • Support engineers in their development through coaching, feedback, and career growth conversations
  • Help evolve engineering practices, ways of working, and operational maturity across the studio
  • Contribute to a culture of continuous improvement, learning, and knowledge sharing

• You have experience leading and developing engineering teams in a complex, distributed environment
  • You are comfortable working with large-scale systems, cloud infrastructure, and platform technologies
  • You know how to balance technical depth with people leadership and team health
  • You have experience partnering with product and cross-functional stakeholders to deliver impact
  • You care about building inclusive teams and creating an environment where everyone can thrive
  • You are experienced in driving delivery while maintaining high standards for quality and reliability
  • You communicate clearly and bring people together around shared goals
  • You have a thoughtful approach to decision-making, trade-offs, and prioritization

• This role is based in Stockholm or London
  • We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! There will be some in person meetings, but still allows for flexibility to work from home.

descriptionPlain:
The Platform team creates the technology that enables Spotify to learn quickly and scale easily, enabling rapid growth in our users and our business around the globe. Spanning many disciplines, we work to make the business work; creating the infrastructure, tooling, frameworks, and capabilities needed to welcome a billion customers.
What if the systems you built changed how millions of developers work, not just at Spotify, but across the industry? The Platform Developer Experience (PDX) team builds the infrastructure and internal products that power how Spotify engineers ship software. Within PDX, the Platform Infrastructure team is building the next generation of cloud and compute foundations that power our internal developer platform and the AI agents running on top of it, and help bring those capabilities to market through Portal, Spotify's external developer platform built on Backstage.
You'll join the Platform Infrastructure team within the Platform Developer Experience (PDX) studio. The team owns the cloud foundations, security posture, and developer tooling that the rest of Platform, and ultimately all of Spotify R&D, depends on. You'll lead a team working at the intersection of cloud infrastructure, platform security, and developer experience.
description:
The Platform team creates the technology that enables Spotify to learn quickly and scale easily, enabling rapid growth in our users and our business around the globe. Spanning many disciplines, we work to make the business work; creating the infrastructure, tooling, frameworks, and capabilities needed to welcome a billion customers.

What if the systems you built changed how millions of developers work, not just at Spotify, but across the industry? The Platform Developer Experience (PDX) team builds the infrastructure and internal products that power how Spotify engineers ship software. Within PDX, the Platform Infrastructure team is building the next generation of cloud and compute foundations that power our internal developer platform and the AI agents running on top of it, and help bring those capabilities to market through Portal, Spotify's external developer platform built on Backstage.

You'll join the Platform Infrastructure team within the Platform Developer Experience (PDX) studio. The team owns the cloud foundations, security posture, and developer tooling that the rest of Platform, and ultimately all of Spotify R&D, depends on. You'll lead a team working at the intersection of cloud infrastructure, platform security, and developer experience.

lists.content:
• Lead and grow a team of AI-native engineers responsible for Platform’s multi-cloud foundation across GCP and AWS, our security posture, and the developer platform that thousands of Spotify engineers depend on every day.

  • Define the technical vision for our cloud platform, and make the architectural calls that shape how the rest of R&D builds, ships, and operates.

  • Own the security and compliance posture of the developer platform end-to-end, ensuring secure-by-default outcomes for every team building on top of us.

  • Drive the evolution of our developer tooling stack (CI/CD, build systems, deployment, observability) so engineers ship safely and quickly along golden paths instead of bespoke setups.

  • Bring AI-native capabilities into platform operations, integrating LLM and agent-based automation into infrastructure changes, security remediation, and developer workflows.

  • Partner with product, go-to-market, and engineering teams to bring our capabilities to market, shaping how the broader industry manages cloud, security, and tooling on top of Portal.

  • Set and enforce production-grade standards for reliability, performance, and cost-efficiency across services operating at Spotify scale.

  • Build a team culture grounded in curiosity, technical rigor, and continuous learning in emerging AI systems.

    •

• You have 3+ years of experience leading engineering teams working on cloud infrastructure, platform engineering, or developer tooling at scale.

  • You have 5+ years of backend or infrastructure engineering experience, with deep expertise in cloud platforms (GCP, AWS).

  • You have a strong grounding in platform security: IAM, secrets management, vulnerability and patch management, and supply chain security.

  • You have solid command of modern developer tooling (CI/CD, build systems, deployment patterns, observability) and an opinion on what makes platform self-service actually work at scale.

  • You have a track record of designing and operating distributed systems where reliability, cost, and security trade-offs are first-class concerns.

  • You're familiar with AI and LLM-based systems, and you have a point of view on how agents and automation can be responsibly applied to infrastructure operations.

  • You communicate clearly across engineers, product, security, and executive stakeholders, explaining complex trade-offs and earning alignment without losing nuance.

  • You create inclusive environments where diverse perspectives strengthen technical and product decisions.

  • You actively seek feedback and invest in growing yourself and your team.

    •

• We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! For this role, you can be within the North Americas region as long as we have a work location.

  • This team operates within the Eastern Standard time zone for collaboration.

    •

description:
We design Spotify’s consumer experience—end to end, moment to moment, across every screen, platform, and partner integration. Our mission is to make listening feel effortless, personal, and joyful for billions of users around the world. That means turning complexity into clarity across hundreds of touchpoints—from our mobile and desktop apps to the smart speakers, TVs, cars, and integrations where Spotify shows up every day. If it touches a consumer, we shape it. We bring deep insight into human behavior, design, and technology to craft experiences that feel intuitive, expressive, and unmistakably Spotify.

We’re building one of Spotify’s most social and engaging experiences: Jam. Jam brings people together through music, enabling friends and groups to listen in real time, wherever they are. It transforms listening from a solo activity into a shared experience and plays an important role in how Spotify connects users through audio.

You’ll join a cross-functional team working across mobile, backend, and platform technologies to shape how millions of people experience music together.

lists.content:
• Lead, support, and grow a team of engineers across iOS, Android, backend, and cross-platform technologies

  • Drive delivery of features that power real-time, shared listening experiences

  • Collaborate closely with Product, Design, and Engineering leaders to shape vision, strategy, and roadmaps

  • Guide the team through complex technical challenges such as real-time synchronization, device connectivity, and cross-platform consistency

  • Enable high-quality, reliable user experiences across devices and platforms

  • Foster a culture of ownership, continuous improvement, and strong engineering practices

  • Create an inclusive and healthy team environment where individuals can grow and do their best work

• You have experience leading and developing engineering teams, including hiring, coaching, and performance management

  • You are experienced with building and scaling complex systems, ideally in real-time or distributed environments

  • You have a strong understanding of software engineering across mobile and backend technologies

  • You care about building inclusive, collaborative teams and creating a positive team environment

  • You are comfortable working across disciplines and influencing product and technical direction

  • You balance execution with long-term thinking and make thoughtful, pragmatic decisions

  • You have experience solving challenges related to synchronization, connectivity, or cross-platform development

• This role is based in London or Stockholm

  • We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! There will be some in person meetings, but still allows for flexibility to work from home.

descriptionPlain:
The Subscription team sits within the broader Subscriptions Mission, focused on helping users seamlessly access, manage, and get the most value from Spotify Premium. We build the systems and experiences that support acquisition, retention, and lifecycle management for millions of users worldwide.
Vantage is the team behind subscription management across Spotify’s web and mobile platforms. We build and operate the core systems that power how millions of Premium users manage and evolve their subscriptions every day. As part of the Freemium Funnel domain and the newly formed Account Management subdomain, we partner closely with Commerce and adjacent teams to create seamless, scalable, and reliable subscription experiences.
description:
The Subscription team sits within the broader Subscriptions Mission, focused on helping users seamlessly access, manage, and get the most value from Spotify Premium. We build the systems and experiences that support acquisition, retention, and lifecycle management for millions of users worldwide.

Vantage is the team behind subscription management across Spotify’s web and mobile platforms. We build and operate the core systems that power how millions of Premium users manage and evolve their subscriptions every day. As part of the Freemium Funnel domain and the newly formed Account Management subdomain, we partner closely with Commerce and adjacent teams to create seamless, scalable, and reliable subscription experiences.

lists.content:
• Lead the technical direction for subscription management systems across backend, web, and mobile surfaces
  • Be accountable for delivery outcomes, system health, and operational reliability at scale
  • Shape the Account Management subdomain strategy and bring clarity to ownership boundaries across teams
  • Drive architectural simplification to improve scalability, maintainability, and developer experience
  • Influence technical direction across the Freemium Funnel domain through strong cross-team collaboration
  • Build and grow a high-performing engineering team grounded in accountability, ownership, and continuous improvement
  • Balance short-term delivery with long-term platform sustainability and evolution

• You have experience leading teams that own complex, multi-surface platform systems across backend, web, and mobile
  • You define and communicate clear technical direction, articulating trade-offs at a strategic level
  • You bring strong architectural judgment and systems thinking to complex problem spaces
  • You operate effectively in ambiguous environments and create clarity across overlapping domains
  • You build empowered engineering teams and foster an environment where people can grow and take ownership
  • You care deeply about system quality, scalability, and operational excellence
  • You balance near-term execution with long-term architectural integrity

• This role is based in New York
  • We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! There will be some in person meetings, but still allows for flexibility to work from home.

descriptionBody:
The Subscription team sits within the broader Subscriptions Mission, focused on helping users seamlessly access, manage, and get the most value from Spotify Premium. We build the systems and experiences that support acquisition, retention, and lifecycle management for millions of users worldwide.

Vantage is the team behind subscription management across Spotify’s web and mobile platforms. We build and operate the core systems that power how millions of Premium users manage and evolve their subscriptions every day. As part of the Freemium Funnel domain and the newly formed Account Management subdomain, we partner closely with Commerce and adjacent teams to create seamless, scalable, and reliable subscription experiences.

descriptionPlain:
The Subscriptions team is part of Spotify’s broader mission to build seamless, scalable experiences that support how users access and pay for Spotify. This includes enabling sustainable growth through reliable, flexible, and innovative subscription and commerce systems.
 
Spotify's Subscriptions Lifecycle Engine (SLE) powers the commerce systems behind every subscription product we offer. From Premium plans to add-ons and new tiers, over 90% of Spotify's revenue flows through this area. You’ll lead teams working at the heart of Spotify’s business, shaping systems that directly impact millions of users and our global revenue engine.

lists.content:
• Lead, support, and grow a team of engineers at different stages of their careers, creating an environment where people can do their best work
  • Own delivery and technical direction for 1–2 squads focused on subscription management, order processing, or third-party integrations
  • Partner closely with Product Managers to shape priorities and translate business goals into clear, actionable engineering plans
  • Ensure reliability and operational excellence for high-scale, revenue-critical systems, including on-call practices and incident response
  • Collaborate with other Engineering Managers across SLE to align on ways of working, technical standards, and team health
  • Navigate compliance requirements such as SOX controls and financial reporting accuracy
  • Contribute to the evolution of Spotify’s commerce platform as we scale our subscriber base and revenue

• You have experience leading software engineering teams, including hiring, coaching, and supporting performance and growth
  • You are experienced with backend or distributed systems and can contribute to technical discussions and architectural decisions
  • You understand how to operate in complex, high-impact domains where changes can affect revenue and financial reporting
  • You are comfortable balancing multiple priorities and working with a range of stakeholders
  • You collaborate effectively in distributed teams and across time zones
  • You care about building inclusive teams and creating an environment where people feel supported and empowered
  • You have worked on e-commerce, payments, or subscription systems, or are excited to learn in this space

• This role is based in London or Stockholm
  • We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! There will be some in person meetings, but still allows for flexibility to work from home

description:
The Subscriptions Mission builds and evolves Spotify’s subscription products to drive sustainable growth by improving conversion, retention, and overall user value. We create seamless, personalized experiences that make it easy for users to discover, upgrade, and stay engaged with Spotify.

Messaging Platform & Tooling is the system that decides what Spotify says to its 700M+ users — and how, when, and where it says it. Nearly every push notification, email, and in-app message flows through this platform, making it one of the highest-leverage systems at Spotify.

Within this space, the Selection squad sits at the core — acting as the decision engine that determines what message reaches each user in real time. You’ll join a high-performing, collaborative team of senior engineers and play a key role in shaping the next generation of this platform as we invest heavily in its future.

lists.content:
• Lead and support the Selection engineering team, focusing on growth, performance, and creating an environment where engineers can do their best work
  • Partner with Product, Data Science, and ML Engineering to define and deliver the technical roadmap for Selection
  • Guide the evolution of Selection from a rules-based system to a more advanced, signal-driven and ML-informed decision engine
  • Deliver platform capabilities that directly impact user engagement, retention, and conversion across Spotify
  • Collaborate across squads and domains to ensure seamless integration and alignment across the Subscriptions mission
  • Represent Selection in planning, strategy discussions, and initiative scoping tied to high-impact business goals
  • Balance speed, scalability, and reliability while navigating complex platform trade-offs that affect millions of users
  • Contribute to the broader engineering culture through hiring, architecture discussions, and cross-team collaboration

    •

• You have 3+ years of experience managing software engineers and have supported teams through growth and delivery
  • You bring a strong sense of ownership and think beyond execution to understand impact, trade-offs, and long-term direction
  • You have experience working with complex systems such as distributed systems, real-time pipelines, or platform infrastructure
  • You care deeply about people and create an environment built on trust, clarity, and continuous development
  • You communicate clearly and effectively with engineers, cross-functional partners, and senior stakeholders
  • You are comfortable engaging in technical discussions and asking thoughtful, probing questions
  • You approach ambiguity with curiosity and are energized by building in evolving and high-impact environments
  • You value collaboration and contribute to an inclusive, supportive team culture

• This role is based in New York
  • We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! There will be some in person meetings, but still allows for flexibility to work from home.

description: You’ll join Spotify’s Global Government Affairs and Public Policy team, and will be based in Washington, DC. This team works closely with policymakers, regulators, and industry partners to help shape policy conversations that impact creators, users, and the broader digital ecosystem. In this role, you’ll act as a key operational partner to senior leadership—helping drive coordination, communication, and execution across a fast-moving and highly visible environment.

lists.content:
• Manage complex, fast-moving calendars across multiple time zones, ensuring leaders are focused on high-priority government and policy engagements

  • Build high-quality presentations and briefing materials for internal leadership, policymakers, and external stakeholders

  • Prepare leaders for meetings by developing agendas, compiling background materials, and ensuring alignment on key messages

  • Coordinate policy meetings, stakeholder engagements, and internal briefings with strong attention to detail

  • Support logistics for external engagements and events in Washington, DC, including coordination with venues, security, and cross-functional partners

  • Support inbox and communications management, prioritizing time-sensitive requests and drafting responses when needed

  • Track key policy priorities, deadlines, and deliverables, ensuring follow-through across multiple workstreams

  • Arrange business travel, including detailed itineraries for policy-related engagements

  • Manage expense reporting and ensure compliance with internal policies

  • Take on operational projects that improve team efficiency, including workflow optimization and documentation

  • Serve as a reliable point of contact for internal teams and external stakeholders, representing Spotify with professionalism and care

• You have 5+ years of experience supporting senior leaders, ideally in government affairs, policy, or a related environment

  • You are highly skilled in building clear, compelling presentations (Google Slides or similar) for executive and external audiences

  • You handle sensitive and confidential information with discretion and strong judgment

  • You communicate clearly and can adapt your style for different audiences, including senior leaders and external stakeholders

  • You are highly organized and comfortable managing multiple priorities in a fast-paced, detail-oriented environment

  • You build strong relationships and collaborate effectively across teams and functions

  • You are proactive and anticipate needs, solving problems before they arise

  • You are comfortable operating across multiple workstreams and connecting the dots between them

  • You manage your time effectively and adjust quickly as priorities shift

  • You are experienced with Google Workspace and collaboration tools such as Slack

  • You are open to occasional travel as needed

  • You bring a steady, reliable approach and thrive in dynamic environments
This role is based in Washington, DC

descriptionPlain:
The Music Vertical connects audiences and artists by powering the full music content lifecycle on Spotify. The team enables creators, distributors, and Spotify teams to ingest, manage, and distribute music at global scale, while delivering the tools and services that support creation, promotion, expression, monetization, and trusted experiences for fans and artists alike.

You’ll join Spotify’s Music team, supporting senior leadership in a fast-moving, highly collaborative environment. This role sits at the center of the team’s day-to-day operations, helping leaders stay focused, prepared, and connected. You’ll play a key part in enabling the team to do their best work while contributing to how we show up internally and externally.

  • Plan and coordinate business travel, including detailed itineraries and logistics
  • Track expenses and manage purchase orders, ensuring deadlines are met
  • Support day-to-day communication, including managing phone logistics and contact updates
  • Organize team events, offsites, and industry moments, and provide onsite support when needed
  • Partner closely with your executive to align their time with team and company priorities
  • Collaborate with communications and content teams to prepare materials for presentations and speaking engagements
  • Support special projects, research, and planning work as needs come up

• You have 3+ years of experience supporting senior leaders in an executive assistant role
  • You know how to stay organized and manage multiple priorities without losing attention to detail
  • You build strong, trusted relationships and handle sensitive information with care
  • You’re comfortable working in a fast-paced environment where priorities can shift quickly
  • You stay calm under pressure and find solutions when plans change
  • You anticipate needs and take action before being asked
  • You balance day-to-day execution with longer-term planning
  • You communicate clearly and work well with a range of partners
  • You enjoy working as part of a team and supporting shared goals

• This role is based in Los Angeles
  • We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! There will be some in person meetings, but still allows for flexibility to work from home.
  • We ask that you come in 2-3 times per week

description: You’ll join Spotify’s Trust & Safety team based in New York. This team plays a critical role in protecting our users, creators, and platform by working across policy, operations, legal, and product to address complex safety challenges. In this role, you’ll act as a key operational partner to senior leadership—supporting high-impact work that often involves sensitive topics, fast decision-making, and cross-functional coordination.

lists.content:
• Manage complex, fast-moving calendars across multiple time zones, ensuring leaders stay focused on critical Trust & Safety priorities

  • Build high-quality presentations and briefing materials for internal leadership, cross-functional partners, and external stakeholders

  • Prepare leaders for meetings by developing agendas, synthesizing inputs, and ensuring clarity on key decisions and actions

  • Support planning and logistics for internal and external Trust & Safety engagements, including sensitive or high-priority discussions

  • Manage inbox communications, prioritizing time-sensitive and confidential matters and drafting responses when needed

  • Track key initiatives, risks, and deadlines, acting as a central coordination point across multiple workstreams

  • Arrange business travel, including detailed itineraries aligned with leadership priorities

  • Manage expense reporting and ensure compliance with internal policies

  • Take on operational projects that improve team workflows, documentation, and efficiency

  • Serve as a trusted point of contact for internal teams and external stakeholders, handling interactions with professionalism and care

• You have 5+ years of experience supporting senior leaders in a fast-paced, high-stakes environment

  • You are highly skilled in building clear, structured presentations (Google Slides or similar) for executive audiences

  • You handle sensitive and confidential information with a high level of discretion and sound judgment

  • You communicate clearly and adapt your approach across different audiences and levels of seniority

  • You are highly organized and comfortable managing multiple priorities with strong attention to detail

  • You build strong, collaborative relationships across teams and functions

  • You take initiative and anticipate needs, solving problems before they arise

  • You are comfortable navigating ambiguity and working across multiple workstreams

  • You manage your time effectively and adapt quickly as priorities shift

  • You are experienced with Google Workspace and collaboration tools such as Slack

  • You are open to occasional travel as needed

  • You bring a calm, steady approach and thrive in dynamic, sometimes high-pressure environments

• This role is based in New York, NY

  • We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! There will be some in person meetings, but still allows for flexibility to work from home.

  • We ask that you come in 1-2 times per month

descriptionPlain: At Spotify, we connect millions of fans to the music and creators they love. Our marketing team brings that connection to life through culture-first storytelling, bold creativity, and experiences that resonate far beyond the app. This role sits within the Marketing team, working at the intersection of music, culture, and fandom to deliver unforgettable experiences across India.
description: At Spotify, we connect millions of fans to the music and creators they love. Our marketing team brings that connection to life through culture-first storytelling, bold creativity, and experiences that resonate far beyond the app. This role sits within the Marketing team, working at the intersection of music, culture, and fandom to deliver unforgettable experiences across India.

lists.content:
• Lead the end-to-end development of experiential marketing programs that bring Spotify to life across physical, digital, and hybrid environments
  • Translate brand strategy into culturally relevant experiences rooted in music, creators, and fandom
  • Partner closely with Music, Brand, Content, Partnerships, and Comms teams to align experiences with broader business priorities
  • Design and execute live music moments including concerts, showcases, fan events, and listening experiences
  • Identify new formats for immersive experiences that blend IRL and digital touchpoints
  • Co-create experiences with brand partners while ensuring Spotify’s creative integrity remains strong
  • Define and track KPIs such as brand lift, engagement, earned media, and fandom growth
  • Manage agency partners and vendors to deliver high-quality, innovative execution
  • Own planning, budgets, timelines, and risk management for complex programs
  • Oversee on-ground execution including production, logistics, talent, and staffing
  • Continuously analyze performance and evolve experiential strategy based on insights

• You have 8+ years of experience in experiential marketing, brand marketing, live events, or partnerships
  • You have led large-scale experiential campaigns or live music events from concept to execution
  • You are experienced working with artists, creators, agencies, and production partners
  • You know how to balance creative ambition with operational excellence and budget discipline
  • You are comfortable navigating ambiguity and influencing across cross-functional teams
  • You have a strong understanding of culture, music, and fandom in India
  • You care deeply about creating inclusive, accessible, and high-quality experiences
  • You are energized by fast-paced environments and bringing ideas to life in the real world
  • You are willing to travel and work non-traditional hours around live events

    •

• This role is based in Mumbai
  • We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! There will be some in person meetings, but still allows for flexibility to work from home.

descriptionBody: At Spotify, we connect millions of fans to the music and creators they love. Our marketing team brings that connection to life through culture-first storytelling, bold creativity, and experiences that resonate far beyond the app. This role sits within the Marketing team, working at the intersection of music, culture, and fandom to deliver unforgettable experiences across India.

descriptionPlain:
At Spotify, we don’t just build marketing campaigns—we create moments that bring fans and artists closer together. Within our K-pop Marketing team, you’ll help shape how K-pop is experienced both on and off the platform. This team sits at the intersection of culture, creativity, and fandom, driving impactful campaigns and live experiences that resonate locally in Korea while influencing global audiences.
Your mission is to bridge the gap between physical live experiences and digital streaming. You will lead the strategy and execution of high-impact activations—ranging from album launches and exclusive artist performances to Spotify’s original programmed events. By straddling the line between innovative concepting and rigorous execution, you will amplify our brand and drive growth for the company.
Based in our Seoul office, you will be a key architect in strengthening the bond between artists and fans, ensuring every activation resonates locally while scaling globally.

lists.content:
• Develop and lead experiential marketing strategies that connect physical fan experiences with Spotify’s digital ecosystem
  • Conceptualize and execute high-impact activations including album launches, artist showcases, and Spotify-led cultural moments
  • Partner with cross-functional teams across marketing, music, creative, comms, media, and analytics to deliver integrated campaigns
  • Collaborate with artists, labels, and external partners to bring culturally relevant and locally resonant ideas to life
  • Own end-to-end production of experiential campaigns, building clear workflows across stakeholders and ensuring seamless execution
  • Use data and insights to measure campaign performance and continuously optimize audience engagement and reach
  • Create compelling presentations and narratives to communicate strategy, performance, and recommendations to senior stakeholders
  • Manage budgets and resources effectively, ensuring high-quality delivery while maximizing impact

• At least 10+ years of experience in experiential, consumer, or integrated marketing within entertainment, music, or lifestyle brands
  • Proven experience leading end-to-end production of live or experiential campaigns, with the ability to scale for broader impact
  • Experience working with artists, creators, agencies, and cross-functional teams in fast-paced environments
  • Strong ability to balance creativity with operational rigor, bringing ideas to life while managing timelines, budgets, and stakeholders
  • Highly organized with the ability to manage multiple priorities while maintaining clear and effective communication
  • Strong collaborator with the ability to align diverse stakeholders toward a shared goal
  • Fluency in Korean and proficiency in English, with strong communication and storytelling skills
  • Deep understanding of K-pop culture and audience behaviors, with a passion for connecting fans and artists
  • Comfortable using tools such as Google Suite, Excel, PowerPoint, or Keynote to communicate ideas and results

• This role is based in Seoul, Korea
  • We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! There will be some in person meetings, but still allows for flexibility to work from home

descriptionPlain:
The Platform team creates the technology that enables Spotify to learn quickly and scale easily, enabling rapid growth in our users and our business around the globe. Spanning many disciplines, we work to make the business work; creating the infrastructure, tooling, frameworks, and capabilities needed to welcome a billion customers.
The Oracles sit at the heart of Spotify’s financial infrastructure, building and evolving the systems that power our subledger and accounting lifecycle. From accruals to reconciliation and ledger integrations, the team is driving a broader FinE initiative to standardize and scale how accounting flows across Spotify. You’ll work closely with engineers, finance partners, and product teams to bring clarity, speed, and accuracy to complex financial systems.
description:
The Platform team creates the technology that enables Spotify to learn quickly and scale easily, enabling rapid growth in our users and our business around the globe. Spanning many disciplines, we work to make the business work; creating the infrastructure, tooling, frameworks, and capabilities needed to welcome a billion customers.

The Oracles sit at the heart of Spotify’s financial infrastructure, building and evolving the systems that power our subledger and accounting lifecycle. From accruals to reconciliation and ledger integrations, the team is driving a broader FinE initiative to standardize and scale how accounting flows across Spotify. You’ll work closely with engineers, finance partners, and product teams to bring clarity, speed, and accuracy to complex financial systems.

lists.content:
• Act as the embedded accounting domain expert within Financial Engineering R&D, contributing to day-to-day product and engineering decisions
  • Guide system and product design to ensure accounting best practices and controls are built into the foundation
  • Translate complex accounting requirements into scalable, structured system inputs across the subledger lifecycle
  • Partner closely with engineers and product teams to shape flows for accruals, adjustments, reconciliation, and rebooking
  • Contribute to the development lifecycle by informing, prototyping, and validating solutions for our core accounting platform
  • Analyze existing accounting flows to separate core requirements from legacy technical constraints
  • Help define and standardize accounting logic across systems like MoneyBooker, IOU Batcher, and NetSuite integrations
  • Break down ambiguous financial and technical challenges into simple, practical solutions

• You have 5+ years of experience in operational or technical accounting within a medium to large organization
  • You have a strong understanding of accrual-based accounting, subledger processes, and accounting best practices
  • You are experienced in partnering with technical teams and can clearly communicate accounting concepts in a systems context
  • You are comfortable working in data-driven or system-heavy environments and are curious about how systems operate
  • You bring a structured, analytical approach to problem solving and are comfortable navigating ambiguity
  • You have experience working with ERP systems, with NetSuite as a plus
  • You are interested in developing your technical skillset and working more closely with engineering
  • You enjoy working at the intersection of finance, systems, and product in evolving environments

• This role is based in Toronto.
  • We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! There will be some in-person meetings, but still allows for flexibility to work from home.

descriptionPlain:
The Rights Systems team builds and operates Spotify Rights Center (SRC), Spotify’s rights management platform that enables the company and its partners to identify, manage, and enforce music licensing rights across creator- and artist-generated content.
Comparable in scope to YouTube’s Content ID, SRC is designed specifically for Spotify’s ecosystem and plays an important role in supporting new formats like video and other emerging content types. Over the past year the team launched SRC from inception to a fully operational production platform, now live and used by real rightsholders.
The platform includes automated content scanning, policy management, enforcement pipelines, dispute workflows, and analytics. The team has grown from a single squad to three squads (approximately 29 engineers, designers, and product specialists) and continues to scale as a strategic investment area for Spotify over the next several years.
description:
The Rights Systems team builds and operates Spotify Rights Center (SRC), Spotify’s rights management platform that enables the company and its partners to identify, manage, and enforce music licensing rights across creator- and artist-generated content.

Comparable in scope to YouTube’s Content ID, SRC is designed specifically for Spotify’s ecosystem and plays an important role in supporting new formats like video and other emerging content types. Over the past year the team launched SRC from inception to a fully operational production platform, now live and used by real rightsholders.

The platform includes automated content scanning, policy management, enforcement pipelines, dispute workflows, and analytics. The team has grown from a single squad to three squads (approximately 29 engineers, designers, and product specialists) and continues to scale as a strategic investment area for Spotify over the next several years.

lists.content:
• Build and evolve the Spotify Rights Center web application used by music labels, publishers, and other rights holders to manage how their content appears on Spotify
  • Collaborate closely with product managers, designers, and backend engineers to deliver intuitive and high-quality user experiences
  • Develop complex workflows that support large datasets, territory-level permissions, and multi-step processes for managing rights and disputes
  • Integrate frontend systems with multiple backend services to support scalable and reliable data flows
  • Write performant, accessible, and well-tested frontend code using modern development practices
  • Partner with backend engineers to design and evolve APIs that support the needs of the product
  • Contribute to product discovery and ideation with engineers, researchers, and product specialists
  • Help improve engineering practices and advocate for strong frontend architecture and quality standards
  • Support a collaborative team environment that encourages learning, experimentation, and growth

• You have experience building modern web applications using TypeScript and React or a similar framework
  • You are comfortable managing complex application state and data flows
  • You have experience integrating frontend systems with APIs such as REST, gRPC, or GraphQL
  • You understand how to design responsive interfaces that handle loading states, pagination, streaming data, and error handling gracefully
  • You care deeply about creating intuitive and accessible user experiences
  • You have experience building enterprise or B2B applications with complex workflows, permissions, and large datasets
  • You write high-quality code and validate it using modern testing approaches such as unit, integration, or end-to-end testing
  • You take ownership of solutions from requirements through deployment and iteration based on user feedback
  • You enjoy learning new domains and problem spaces and collaborating with teammates to solve meaningful problems

• This role is based in London or Stockholm
  • We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! There will be some in person meetings, but still allows for flexibility to work from home

descriptionPlain:
Our mission on the Advertising Product & Technology team is to build a next generation advertising platform that aligns with our unique value proposition for audio and video. We work to scale the user experience for hundreds of millions of fans and hundreds of thousands of advertisers. This scale brings unique challenges as well as tremendous opportunities for our artists and creators.


Spotify is the world’s leading audio streaming platform, serving millions of listeners across 184 markets. We aim to encourage creative artists to support themselves through art and offer fans the opportunity to be encouraged. Within Spotify, the Ads Platform team empowers brands and advertisers to connect with engaged, passionate listeners at scale.
 
Joining Ads RnD as a versatile engineer, you’ll become part of a dynamic, collaborative team passionate about crafting innovative self-serve advertising tools. You will work alongside Product Managers, Designers, Data Scientists, and senior engineers—rapidly learning our stack, shipping end to end features, and iterating on experiments to fuel Spotify Ad platform’s growth.
description:
Our mission on the Advertising Product & Technology team is to build a next generation advertising platform that aligns with our unique value proposition for audio and video. We work to scale the user experience for hundreds of millions of fans and hundreds of thousands of advertisers. This scale brings unique challenges as well as tremendous opportunities for our artists and creators.

Spotify is the world’s leading audio streaming platform, serving millions of listeners across 184 markets. We aim to encourage creative artists to support themselves through art and offer fans the opportunity to be encouraged. Within Spotify, the Ads Platform team empowers brands and advertisers to connect with engaged, passionate listeners at scale.
 
Joining Ads RnD as a versatile engineer, you’ll become part of a dynamic, collaborative team passionate about crafting innovative self-serve advertising tools. You will work alongside Product Managers, Designers, Data Scientists, and senior engineers—rapidly learning our stack, shipping end to end features, and iterating on experiments to fuel Spotify Ad platform’s growth.

lists.content:
• Design, implement and ship features using Java, Typescript and other technologies for the Spotify Ads Manager.
  • Partner with product managers and business stakeholders to build and scale our services, powering our platform.
  • Collaborate with teams and disciplines across the world to deliver high-quality, high-impact customer-facing products and features.
  • Perform code reviews, create documentation and take on complex bug fixes across services and multiple levels of the stack.
  • Improve operational excellence and share your knowledge across the company to promote best practices.
  • Work in an environment that supports your individual growth.

• You are a full-stack engineer with at least 3 years of hands-on experience in Java and TypeScript to implement end-to-end features that handle a high volume of data.
  • You have a strong understanding of web and API development principles and significant experience in building, integrating with, and using APIs. Proficiency in understanding and implementing REST, gRPC, and GraphQL is essential.
  • You care about observability and building robust & reliable systems. This includes practical experience with engineering best practices, testing automation (unit, integration, and end-to-end), and monitoring tools to ensure software quality and system stability.
  • You own your solutions end-to-end, from scoping tickets through to deployment and smoke testing your features in production.
  • Nice to have experience with experimentation platforms, A/B testing concepts, and proficiency in SQL.
  • You care about software architecture across the front-end, back-end, and the APIs gluing them together.
  • You have experience working directly with stakeholders and Product leadership. You’re not just coding—you’re moving adoption, revenue, and user happiness metrics.
  • Embracing challenges and celebrating wins, as well as failures, to derive findings, is a key aspect of your approach.
  • You are comfortable working in an agile environment and are comfortable challenging yourself and your team to improve their ways of working.

• You’ll be based in Australia, within a region in which we have a work location (Sydney or Melbourne)
  • We are a distributed workforce enabling our band members to find a work mode that is best for them! We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! There will be some in person meetings, but still allows for flexibility to work from home full time. 
  • Working hours? We operate within the Australian Eastern Standard time zone for collaboration and often need to communicate with our other team members in US, PST and EST

This scale brings unique challenges as well as tremendous opportunities for our artists and creators.

Spotify is the world’s leading audio streaming platform, serving millions of listeners across 184 markets. We aim to encourage creative artists to support themselves through art and offer fans the opportunity to be encouraged. Within Spotify, the Ads Platform team empowers brands and advertisers to connect with engaged, passionate listeners at scale.
 
Joining Ads RnD as a versatile engineer, you’ll become part of a dynamic, collaborative team passionate about crafting innovative self-serve advertising tools. You will work alongside Product Managers, Designers, Data Scientists, and senior engineers—rapidly learning our stack, shipping end to end features, and iterating on experiments to fuel Spotify Ad platform’s growth.
