descriptionPlain:
Our mission on the Advertising Product & Technology team is to build a next generation advertising platform that aligns with our unique value proposition for audio and video. We work to scale the user experience for hundreds of millions of fans and hundreds of thousands of advertisers. This scale brings unique challenges as well as tremendous opportunities for our artists and creators.


Spotify is hiring a Full Stack Engineer to join the Spotify Advertising Team. Our team is responsible for maintaining our Ads API and primary data store: if you’re passionate about data availability, integrity and performance at scale, come join us!
description:
Our mission on the Advertising Product & Technology team is to build a next generation advertising platform that aligns with our unique value proposition for audio and video. We work to scale the user experience for hundreds of millions of fans and hundreds of thousands of advertisers. This scale brings unique challenges as well as tremendous opportunities for our artists and creators.

Spotify is hiring a Full Stack Engineer to join the Spotify Advertising Team. Our team is responsible for maintaining our Ads API and primary data store: if you’re passionate about data availability, integrity and performance at scale, come join us!

lists.content:
• Collaborate with band members on the Ads API and Data Store team to enhance our API and data ecosystem, including ingestion, consumption, transformation and storage.
  • Collaborate with engineering squads across Spotify, helping them enhance their products by providing first class data solutions, knowledge and education.
  • Work in an environment that supports your individual growth
  • Improve operational excellence and share your knowledge across the company to promote best practices.

• You’re an experienced Java engineer with 5+ years of working experience on backend services as scale.
  • You have built and maintained APIs at scale.
  • You have some experience with Data Engineering with Scala.
  • Additional tools and technologies such as Spring, GCP, NextJS, GraphQL are a plus.
  • You have a strong grasp of design patterns, data structures, and distributed architecture.
  • You care about observability & reliability and have experience with engineering best practices and testing automation to build quality into software and systems.
  • You value team success over personal success and have the ability to both uplift and up-level other engineers.

• We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! For this role, it can be within the North America region in which we have a work location.
  • This team collaborates across the Pacific time zone, Australian, and Eastern time zone.

descriptionPlain:
Spotify for Authors (SFA) is the creator platform for the audiobook industry — a web application that gives publishers, authors, and narrators tools to manage their catalog, track   performance, and distribute content on Spotify. We're scaling toward 100,000 users across a React/TypeScript frontend, Java backend services, and data pipelines that feed into Spotify's   broader audiobook ecosystem.
SFA connects to Spotify's highest-priority bets: expanding audiobook access, building content creation tools, and shaping discovery surfaces. We operate with a "short toes" philosophy —   contribution has no borders, quality is shared, and we use modern tools (including AI) to reduce toil and amplify creativity.

lists.content:
• Build and evolve features end-to-end across a React/TypeScript frontend and Java backend services that power the SFA platform at scale
  • Use AI-assisted development tools as a core part of your workflow for code generation, automated testing, and AI-powered code review
  • Deliver testable, maintainable, and high-quality code and take operational responsibility for the components you develop
  • Collaborate closely with engineers, product managers, and designers to ship impactful experiences for audiobook creators
  • Contribute to technical discussions and participate in RFCs and code reviews
  • Share knowledge and promote best practices within your squad

• You have experience building web applications across the stack. TypeScript and Java experience preferred.
  • You're motivated to learn, take on new challenges, and T-shape
  • You think product-first while building tech solutions
  • You're comfortable using AI tools and agents to support your development workflow
  • You're comfortable with modern infrastructure practices (CI/CD, containers, cloud platforms) and care about observability
  • You communicate clearly and collaborate well in a distributed team
  • You're pragmatic and motivated by solving real problems for creators

• This role is based in Stockholm or London
  • We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! There will be some in person meetings, but still allows for flexibility to work from home.

description:
Podcast R&D is a product and technology team within Podcast Vertical at Spotify, focused on building best-in-class tools for creators. We own the platforms, tools, and features that power the creator experience allowing podcasters and publishers to host, create, and distribute their content, grow and engage their audiences, and earn revenue on Spotify.

This Backend leaning Full Stack role is part of the New Value area, a cross-functional team working across business, product, design, and insights to identify, evaluate, launch, and scale new content verticals and revenue models. Our ambition is to evolve the Podcast Vertical into a general-purpose creator and publisher platform that delivers meaningful value to Spotify Premium and drives over $1B in incremental revenue.

You’ll play a key role within a diverse, distributed squad focused on subscriptions and fandom experiences - including Synced Feeds, the creator tooling that drives subscription awareness and conversion on Spotify; the Spotify Open Access API (SOA), which enables third-party - hosted subscriptions; and new first-party products in the subscription content space. In the year ahead, the squad will focus on building a new, scalable version of Spotify’s native subscription offering, enhancing Synced Feeds, and evolving SOA. Put together, these efforts are establishing a strong foundation for creators of all sizes to monetize their fans directly on Spotify.

lists.content:
• Design, build and maintain scalable backend services (Java)
  • Be an active part of creating and evolving APIs and services that connect user experiences with internal scalable infrastructure, while applying product requirements
  • Drive improvements to system architecture, performance, and developer experience
  • Develop and enhance our user-facing layer and frontend SDKs (React/Typescript)
  • Collaborate closely with a talented group of engineers, product managers, designers and data specialists across multiple teams
  • Contribute to technical discussions and help shape platform decisions

• You are experienced in designing and developing backend applications in Java.
  • You have a solid understanding of React, and TypeScript and are passionate about delivering end-to-end experiences and software architecture across the back-end, front-end, and the APIs that glue them together.
  • You have a growth mindset, are curious, and are not afraid to be experimental. You share your ideas and continuously improve yourself and the team around you.
  • Experience with infrastructure-related technologies is a plus.
  • You care about agile software development and a culture of constant learning and improvement.
  • You have excellent analytical and problem-solving skills and can communicate your ideas.
  • You have experience and passion for mentoring and encouraging collaborative teams.

• We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! For this role, you can be within the North Americas region as long as we have a work location.
  • This team operates within the Eastern Standard time zone for collaboration.

descriptionPlain:
UI Studio is Spotify’s internal generative UI prototyping platform that enables teams to spin up fully functioning replicas of Spotify surfaces — complete with playable music — to explore and pitch new product ideas. What started as a small internal project has evolved into a widely adopted platform, and we’re now expanding it into a full generative UI system where teams can prompt for the interfaces they need.
We’re looking for a Fullstack Engineer who is strong across backend systems and confident building high-quality frontend experiences — to help scale the platform’s technical foundations and AI-native capabilities. You’ll play a meaningful role in shaping architecture, evolving core systems, and delivering reliable, developer-facing platform functionality used across Spotify.
description:
UI Studio is Spotify’s internal generative UI prototyping platform that enables teams to spin up fully functioning replicas of Spotify surfaces — complete with playable music — to explore and pitch new product ideas. What started as a small internal project has evolved into a widely adopted platform, and we’re now expanding it into a full generative UI system where teams can prompt for the interfaces they need.

We’re looking for a Fullstack Engineer who is strong across backend systems and confident building high-quality frontend experiences — to help scale the platform’s technical foundations and AI-native capabilities. You’ll play a meaningful role in shaping architecture, evolving core systems, and delivering reliable, developer-facing platform functionality used across Spotify.

lists.content:
• Design and deliver fullstack features that power UI Studio’s generative platform capabilities
  • Architect and implement scalable services and user-facing workflows using TypeScript across frontend and backend (Node.js)
  • Integrate LLM-powered coding agents into product workflows, including tool use, planning loops, evaluation loops, and multi-step orchestration
  • Contribute to search and retrieval infrastructure, including vector-based search systems, indexing strategies, and relevance optimization
  • Design, ship, and operate distributed systems deployed via containerized infrastructure such as Docker and Kubernetes
  • Take end-to-end ownership of well-scoped systems and features, from technical design and implementation through deployment, monitoring, and operational support
  • Improve internal APIs, platform primitives, and developer tooling to create intuitive and scalable platform experiences
  • Uphold strong engineering standards through automated testing, observability, documentation, and continuous delivery practices

• You have professional experience building production systems with TypeScript and Node.js, and you’re comfortable contributing across the full stack
  • You have hands-on experience building or meaningfully integrating LLM-powered coding agents, including tool integrations and multi-step workflows
  • You understand search and retrieval design patterns, such as vector search systems, Elasticsearch, and indexing strategies
  • You have a passion for (and preferably experience working with) Design Systems
  • You have experience designing and operating distributed systems and deploying services using containerized tooling such as Docker and Kubernetes
  • You demonstrate strong software engineering fundamentals and can independently ship well-scoped features end-to-end
  • You think in systems — considering reliability, scalability, observability, and maintainability in your designs
  • You communicate clearly in writing and produce structured, actionable technical documentation
  • You care about developer experience and have experience building tools or platforms used by other engineers

• This role is based in Stockholm
  • We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! There will be some in person meetings, but still allows for flexibility to work from home.

descriptionPlain:
The Platform team creates the technology that enables Spotify to learn quickly and scale easily, enabling rapid growth in our users and our business around the globe. Spanning many disciplines, we work to make the business work; creating the infrastructure, tooling, frameworks, and capabilities needed to welcome a billion customers.
You’ll join the Platform team, focused on building AI-native conversational analytics experiences that help teams across Spotify explore, understand, and act on data more intuitively. We’re rethinking how people interact with analytics by combining modern fullstack engineering with LLM-powered interfaces. This is a highly collaborative environment where engineers, product, and data partners work closely to shape the future of internal data experiences.

lists.content:
• Build and ship end-to-end features for conversational analytics products, from backend services to intuitive frontend interfaces
  • Design and develop AI-native workflows that allow users to query and explore data using natural language
  • Collaborate with product managers, designers, and data teams to turn complex problems into simple, scalable solutions
  • Contribute to system architecture decisions, ensuring performance, reliability, and scalability
  • Integrate LLMs and other AI capabilities into production systems in a responsible and thoughtful way
  • Improve developer experience and tooling to enable faster iteration and experimentation
  • Take ownership of features from idea through launch and iteration, focusing on real user impact

• You have experience building fullstack applications using modern frameworks and technologies
  • You are comfortable working across backend and frontend systems, and enjoy owning problems end-to-end
  • You have experience with APIs, distributed systems, and data-driven applications
  • You understand how to design clean, maintainable, and scalable code
  • You are curious about AI/ML and have worked with or are excited to work with LLM-powered systems
  • You care about user experience and strive to make complex workflows feel simple and intuitive
  • You have 3+ years of professional software engineering experience
  • You collaborate effectively with cross-functional partners and value shared ownership

• This role is based in Stockholm
  • We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! There will be some in person meetings, but still allows for flexibility to work from home.

descriptionPlain:
Spotify’s Global Experiential and Content Production team is seeking an Associate Director with well-rounded content and event production experience to join our team.
While this role sits within Global Communications, the impact is company-wide. You will support multiple functions across the business including Communications, Ads, Government Affairs, Product, Music, Creator, Podcast and Audiobooks teams—to bring their initiatives to life through strategic events and content.
Importantly: this team works alongside Spotify’s Brand Experiential Marketing team. That team owns global consumer and brand marketing events. This team owns a defined set of experiential programs tied to communications priorities, creator/industry relationships, policy moments, company events, VIP/Influencer programming, thought leadership platforms, and other business-driven needs - not all events across Spotify.
As an Associate Director, you will play a key role in delivering high-impact creative content and managing live event production. You will build scalable solutions to maximize the success of these projects and support GECP’s Director in the development, management, and execution of our team objectives and project goals. This is a senior creative and production role, guiding both the experiential work and content creation. 
The role requires significant travel (domestic and international) up to 70%
description:
Spotify’s Global Experiential and Content Production team is seeking an Associate Director with well-rounded content and event production experience to join our team.

While this role sits within Global Communications, the impact is company-wide. You will support multiple functions across the business including Communications, Ads, Government Affairs, Product, Music, Creator, Podcast and Audiobooks teams—to bring their initiatives to life through strategic events and content.

Importantly: this team works alongside Spotify’s Brand Experiential Marketing team. That team owns global consumer and brand marketing events. This team owns a defined set of experiential programs tied to communications priorities, creator/industry relationships, policy moments, company events, VIP/Influencer programming, thought leadership platforms, and other business-driven needs - not all events across Spotify.

As an Associate Director, you will play a key role in delivering high-impact creative content and managing live event production. You will build scalable solutions to maximize the success of these projects and support GECP’s Director in the development, management, and execution of our team objectives and project goals. This is a senior creative and production role, guiding both the experiential work and content creation. 

The role requires significant travel (domestic and international) up to 70%

lists.content:
• Lead the planning and delivery of live experiences including corporate programs, industry activations, creator and partner events, policy engagements, internal events, and VIP experiences
  • Shape end-to-end production, from concept through execution, ensuring high-quality creative and operational delivery
  • Bring strong expertise in stage production, including lighting, audio, and production design, to elevate each experience
  • Manage vendor and agency partnerships, selecting the right collaborators based on scale, complexity, and creative needs
  • Oversee content capture shoots, partnering with internal stakeholders from early development through on-site execution
  • Guide pre- and post-production workflows to deliver content on time and within budget
  • Align teams on creative direction, ensuring clarity across visual storytelling and editorial decisions
  • Build and manage budgets, timelines, and approval processes across multiple concurrent projects
  • Navigate legal and licensing requirements related to visuals, talent, and music usage
  • Partner with cross-functional senior leaders to bring ideas to life and solve complex business challenges
  • Contribute to building scalable processes, tools, and workflows across the team
  • Mentor and support producers while leading large-scale, high-visibility productions

• You have 10+ years of experience in event, show, and content production
  • You are experienced in leading large-scale live productions and delivering high-quality creative outcomes
  • You know how to translate business goals into engaging, well-crafted experiences
  • You have a strong understanding of content production workflows, including pre- and post-production processes
  • You are experienced in managing agencies, vendors, and cross-functional stakeholders across global teams
  • You have a deep understanding of staging, lighting, fabrication, and production design
  • You are comfortable managing budgets, contracts, and financial reconciliation
  • You communicate clearly and can navigate complex conversations with a range of stakeholders
  • You bring a global mindset and can collaborate effectively across regions and time zones
  • You care about building inclusive, collaborative environments and supporting diverse teams and partners
  • You are able to balance creative thinking with strong organization and prioritization skills

• This role is based in New York City
  • We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! There will be some in person meetings, but still allows for flexibility to work from home. We ask that you come in 2-3 times per week.

descriptionPlain:
We are seeking a dynamic, hard-working, strategic candidate to join Spotify’s Global Government Affairs team in Istanbul as Government Affairs Manager for Türkiye. While this role is primarily focused on Türkiye, it will also contribute to public policy work across other markets in the Middle East North Africa Pakistan (MENAP) region.
 
The Government Affairs team is charged with understanding the global and local policy landscapes in relation to Spotify’s business objectives and advocating on the company’s behalf on critical issues. One of our key roles is to promote Spotify’s ability to compete fairly and grow by influencing public policies. We represent the company before legislators and regulators, anticipate emerging issues, and mitigate potential risks.
 
The Manager of Türkiye Government Affairs will work as part of a global government affairs team, coordinating closely with colleagues on the team, as well as other groups throughout Spotify to craft effective, consistent positions on the issues of greatest importance to our company. 
 
This position will report to the Head of Government Affairs for Middle East Africa (MEA) and Japan-Asia Pacific (JAPAC) based in Dubai.
description:
We are seeking a dynamic, hard-working, strategic candidate to join Spotify’s Global Government Affairs team in Istanbul as Government Affairs Manager for Türkiye. While this role is primarily focused on Türkiye, it will also contribute to public policy work across other markets in the Middle East North Africa Pakistan (MENAP) region.
 
The Government Affairs team is charged with understanding the global and local policy landscapes in relation to Spotify’s business objectives and advocating on the company’s behalf on critical issues. One of our key roles is to promote Spotify’s ability to compete fairly and grow by influencing public policies. We represent the company before legislators and regulators, anticipate emerging issues, and mitigate potential risks.
 
The Manager of Türkiye Government Affairs will work as part of a global government affairs team, coordinating closely with colleagues on the team, as well as other groups throughout Spotify to craft effective, consistent positions on the issues of greatest importance to our company. 
 
This position will report to the Head of Government Affairs for Middle East Africa (MEA) and Japan-Asia Pacific (JAPAC) based in Dubai.

lists.content:
• Engage regularly with policymakers in Türkiye, including members of the Grand National Assembly, relevant ministries, regulators, and key government stakeholders, representing Spotify’s perspective on digital economy and creative sector issues
  • Build deep expertise on public policy topics critical to Spotify in Türkiye, including music policy, content moderation and online safety, media regulation, digital taxation, copyright and licensing, digital competition policy, and data protection and privacy
  • Identify, assess, and prioritize public policy developments affecting Spotify in Türkiye, and design thoughtful, effective advocacy strategies in partnership with global and regional colleagues
  • Contribute to public policy initiatives across other MENA markets, working in close coordination with regional and global Government Affairs teams
  • Collaborate with internal teams such as Communications, Trust & Safety, Legal, Product, and Market teams to align global policy positions with local realities and business goals
  • Manage and coordinate the work of external consultants and policy advisors in Türkiye, ensuring consistency with Spotify’s global strategy and high-quality stakeholder engagement
  • Build constructive relationships with external stakeholders including trade associations, civil society organizations, academic experts, and members of the creative community to support thought leadership and coalition-building

• You have experience developing and executing public policy or government affairs strategies in complex regulatory environments
  • You are comfortable working with government institutions, regulators, trade associations, and other public policy stakeholders
  • You bring experience from government, a company, a trade association, or a combination of these environments
  • You understand public policy issues affecting technology, digital platforms, media, or the creative economy
  • You are able to think strategically while also diving into details to solve problems and move work forward
  • You build trust easily and can work effectively across cultures, functions, and seniority levels
  • You communicate clearly and persuasively, both in writing and verbally, in Turkish and English
  • You approach advocacy with integrity, professionalism, and a collaborative mindset
  • You are open to travel as needed and comfortable working across multiple markets

• This role is based in Türkiye, with travel expected to markets and when needed. This is not a remote position.
    •

descriptionPlain:
Spotify is looking for a Growth Manager to help accelerate user and subscriber growth in Japan. This role will play a key part in shaping and executing Japan-specific growth strategies, working closely with cross-functional partners both locally and globally.
As a Growth Manager, you will lead end-to-end growth initiatives — from strategic planning through execution and performance analysis — while navigating a highly collaborative and matrixed environment.
You’ll combine strong analytical thinking with curiosity and adaptability, helping Spotify continue to grow in one of its most unique and dynamic markets.
description:
Spotify is looking for a Growth Manager to help accelerate user and subscriber growth in Japan. This role will play a key part in shaping and executing Japan-specific growth strategies, working closely with cross-functional partners both locally and globally.

As a Growth Manager, you will lead end-to-end growth initiatives — from strategic planning through execution and performance analysis — while navigating a highly collaborative and matrixed environment.

You’ll combine strong analytical thinking with curiosity and adaptability, helping Spotify continue to grow in one of its most unique and dynamic markets.

lists.content:
• Own the growth strategy for Japan, defining market-level priorities, success metrics, and trade-offs across the user lifecycle in partnership with regional and global teams

  • Translate global and regional growth priorities into locally relevant strategies informed by data and market insights

  • Lead the planning and execution of growth campaigns, ensuring high-quality delivery from campaign initiation through execution, optimization, and post-campaign analysis

  • Partner closely with cross-functional teams such as Marketing, Music, Analytics, Strategy & Operations, Product, Legal, and Finance to deliver aligned and effective initiatives

  • Manage key stakeholders across Japan and the region, clearly communicating strategy, progress, results, and learnings

  • Own ongoing performance management for the Japan market, synthesizing results across initiatives to identify trends, opportunities, and strategic implications beyond individual campaigns

  • Contribute to a strong learning culture by documenting results and sharing insights across teams and markets

• You have a minimum of 5+ years of experience in growth, Product marketing, strategy, or a related role within a consumer, digital, or subscription-based business

  • You’re comfortable owning initiatives end-to-end, balancing strategic thinking with hands-on execution

  • You are highly analytical and data-informed, with strong critical and structured thinking skills

  • You enjoy working across diverse teams and functions, and can adapt your communication style to different stakeholders

  • You’re curious, open-minded, and motivated to learn — able to thrive in ambiguous and fast-moving environments

  • You communicate clearly and thoughtfully, both verbally and in writing

  • You are fluent in Japanese and English

• This role is based in Tokyo
  • We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! There will be some in person meetings, but still allows for flexibility to work from home. We ask that you come in 3 times per week

descriptionPlain:
The Platform team creates the technology that enables Spotify to learn quickly and scale easily, enabling rapid growth in our users and our business around the globe. Spanning many disciplines, we work to make the business work; creating the infrastructure, tooling, frameworks, and capabilities needed to welcome a billion customers.
 
About the Team
Backstage was created at Spotify and has grown into a leading SaaS and open source platform for developer portals. Thousands of companies use Backstage to bring structure to complex microservices environments, improve developer experience, and ship high-quality software faster — including in the age of AI — without losing autonomy or control.Backstage powers centralized software catalogs, standards, and AI enablement capabilities across open source, plugins, and SaaS offerings.
 
Our customers include global enterprises and Fortune 100 companies transforming their engineering culture. This role leads marketing for Shipping Our Expertise (SOE), Spotify’s B2B SaaS business built on our platform capabilities, while also shaping the broader Spotify R&D brand.

Our customers include global enterprises and Fortune 100 companies transforming their engineering culture. This role leads marketing for Shipping Our Expertise (SOE), Spotify’s B2B SaaS business built on our platform capabilities, while also shaping the broader Spotify R&D brand.

lists.content:
• Set and drive the end-to-end marketing strategy for the SOE business across product marketing, growth, and brand
  • Build and execute growth strategies that drive measurable revenue, adoption, and customer expansion
  • Lead digital and paid acquisition programs targeting developers, engineering leaders, and enterprise buyers
  • Define clear positioning and differentiated messaging for technical B2B audiences
  • Partner closely with Engineering, Product, and Sales to align roadmap, go-to-market plans, and commercial priorities
  • Establish and track full-funnel performance across awareness, acquisition, activation, retention, and revenue
  • Lead event and community strategy to increase visibility and generate qualified pipeline
  • Scale marketing operations through strong planning, dashboards, and performance reporting
  • Build, mentor, and grow a high-performing, data-informed marketing team

• You have 10+ years of experience in B2B SaaS, growth marketing, or product marketing
  • You have 5+ years of experience leading and developing high-performing teams
  • You have experience marketing developer tools, infrastructure platforms, or technical SaaS products
  • You understand engineering culture and know how to communicate with technical audiences
  • You have owned revenue or adoption targets and can demonstrate measurable business impact
  • You have led digital performance marketing, paid acquisition, and growth funnel optimization
  • You are comfortable working with data, building forecasts, and using dashboards to guide decisions
  • You balance commercial thinking with strong product intuition and user-centered mindset
  • You bring structure and clarity to complex, ambiguous initiatives
  • You thrive in fast-moving environments and enjoy building new approaches from the ground up

• This role is based in Toronto
  • We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! There will be some in person meetings, but still allows for flexibility to work from home.

descriptionPlain:
You will join Spotify’s Global Government Affairs team, working at the intersection of policy, technology, and creativity. This team partners with governments, regulators, and industry groups around the world to help shape a fair, innovative, and inclusive digital ecosystem.
In Australia and New Zealand, you’ll play a key role in representing Spotify’s voice on issues that matter to creators, listeners, and the broader media landscape—ranging from competition and online safety to AI and the future of the creative economy.
description:
You will join Spotify’s Global Government Affairs team, working at the intersection of policy, technology, and creativity. This team partners with governments, regulators, and industry groups around the world to help shape a fair, innovative, and inclusive digital ecosystem.

In Australia and New Zealand, you’ll play a key role in representing Spotify’s voice on issues that matter to creators, listeners, and the broader media landscape—ranging from competition and online safety to AI and the future of the creative economy.

lists.content:
• Lead Spotify’s government affairs strategy across Australia and New Zealand, building trusted relationships with policymakers and stakeholders
  • Represent Spotify in meetings with government officials, regulators, and industry groups across both markets
  • Shape and influence public policy on issues impacting the digital, media, and creator ecosystem
  • Provide clear, strategic guidance to senior leaders on political, legislative, and regulatory developments
  • Partner with Legal, Communications, Music and business teams to align on priorities and messaging
  • Monitor policy trends and proactively identify risks and opportunities for Spotify, creators, and users
  • Develop advocacy strategies and positions that reflect Spotify’s values and support a sustainable, inclusive creative economy
  • Travel regularly between Sydney and Canberra to maintain strong, in-person engagement

• You have significant experience in government affairs, public policy, or a related field across Australia and New Zealand
  • You are experienced in building relationships with government officials, regulators, and industry stakeholders
  • You have a strong understanding of the ANZ political and regulatory landscape, including legislative processes
  • You know how to turn complex policy topics into clear, actionable insights
  • You are comfortable advising senior leaders and contributing to strategic decision-making
  • You care about creating a fair, inclusive, and forward-looking environment for creators, platforms, and users
  • You communicate clearly and represent organizations externally with credibility and authenticity
  • You are able to manage multiple priorities in a fast-moving environment with sound judgment

• This role can be based in Sydney or Canberra

    •

descriptionPlain:
We are looking for a HR Generalist to join our Global HR team! This is a fundamental role in the HR team and will be central to ensuring HR operational efficiency and effectiveness along with supporting the business locally. This role will be responsible for HR within the Japan and Korea markets, ensuring to stay connected with the broader JAPAC/SAMEA team along with the Global HR team.
 
In this role, you will support all stages of the employee lifecycle including delivery of employee onboarding, facilitating training, Workday processing, working to resolve employee queries, managing employee relations cases, and managing local benefits. You'll partner with the local business leaders along with HR Business Partners, Employment Legal, Talent Acquisition, Payroll, Benefits, and others to deliver world-class employee support to the Japan and Korea markets.
description:
We are looking for a HR Generalist to join our Global HR team! This is a fundamental role in the HR team and will be central to ensuring HR operational efficiency and effectiveness along with supporting the business locally. This role will be responsible for HR within the Japan and Korea markets, ensuring to stay connected with the broader JAPAC/SAMEA team along with the Global HR team.
 
In this role, you will support all stages of the employee lifecycle including delivery of employee onboarding, facilitating training, Workday processing, working to resolve employee queries, managing employee relations cases, and managing local benefits. You'll partner with the local business leaders along with HR Business Partners, Employment Legal, Talent Acquisition, Payroll, Benefits, and others to deliver world-class employee support to the Japan and Korea markets.

lists.content:
• Manage all Workday processing and employee questions from onboarding to off-boarding for all employees in Japan and Korea
  • Be the local specialist providing hands-on labor law advice and support for Japan and Korea
  • Partner with the JAPAC/SAMEA HR Business Partner, Employment Legal, Talent Acquisition, Payroll, Benefits, and many others to understand and resolve local issues
  • Update and adjust processes and content, if needed, based on new local labor laws
  • Handle all local documents and government paperwork required for Japan and Korea
  • Manage projects with various stakeholders to improve and streamline processes, ensuring we drive global consistency along with adjusting for local nuances as needed
  • Support the JAPAC/SAMEA HR Business Partner to provide local support to the Japan and Korea business leaders
  • Delivering training to support manager development
  • Reviewing HR processes and suggesting changes to improve efficiency and scalability
  • You will be located at the Tokyo office
  • Ability to travel is required

• You have at least 5 years of experience working in an HR Generalist, Employee Relations Manager, People Operations, or HR Manager role
  • Global experience working with international counterparts for an organization that has offices around the world
  • Have strong knowledge of Japanese and Korean local labor regulations and employment practices as well as a desire to learn more
  • Have experience working in a matrixed organization, with experience working in a media or technology company a plus
  • Have exposure or experience with compensation and benefits, with an understanding of the local market a plus
  • Ability to manage employee relations cases, such as performance management, harassment, discrimination, and other policy violations, from intake through investigation
  • Ability to work collaboratively, being inclusive of all partners
  • Ability to work independently and comfortable working in a distributed setting
  • Ability to productively challenge and influence other teams, managers, and leaders
  • Ability to prioritize well and be flexible in a fast-paced environment with changing demands where being resourceful to find answers is key
  • Strong communication skills - both verbal and written
  • Demonstrate a solution-focused approach to problem-solving, with the ability to find pragmatic outcomes
  • Fluency in Japanese and English. Basic or intermediate knowledge of Korean would be an added advantage

• This role is based in Tokyo, Japan.
  • We ask that you come into the office 2 to 3 times per week.

descriptionPlain: The Lead, Music Development acts as the principal architect in fortifying Spotify’s relationships and standing within the Turkish music landscape across labels, industry bodies, and other institutional stakeholders. Acting as the spokesperson, this role strengthens Spotify’s position in the Turkish music ecosystem by shaping clear narratives and leading strategic initiatives and events. You will address industry sentiment, focus on strengthening institutional relationships, and connect with internal global music leadership to ensure a seamless flow of information and feedback.
description: The Lead, Music Development acts as the principal architect in fortifying Spotify’s relationships and standing within the Turkish music landscape across labels, industry bodies, and other institutional stakeholders. Acting as the spokesperson, this role strengthens Spotify’s position in the Turkish music ecosystem by shaping clear narratives and leading strategic initiatives and events. You will address industry sentiment, focus on strengthening institutional relationships, and connect with internal global music leadership to ensure a seamless flow of information and feedback.

lists.content:
• Build and lead relationships with major and independent labels, CMOs, and key music industry partners across Türkiye
  • Drive strategic alignment through initiatives like Quarterly Business Reviews, focusing on long-term growth and shared outcomes
  • Represent Spotify as a trusted voice in the Turkish music ecosystem through events, panels, and industry forums
  • Shape and deliver clear, compelling narratives that communicate Spotify’s platform, values, and innovations
  • Partner with Marketing, PR, Government Affairs, and global Music teams to align communication and engagement strategies
  • Provide local teams with insights on market trends, emerging genres, and shifts in listener behavior
  • Translate market data into actionable strategies that support artists, partners, and business growth
  • Design and lead education initiatives that help partners better understand Spotify’s tools, model, and opportunities
  • Define and deliver initiatives that support the growth, diversity, and global reach of Turkish music
  • Manage budgets to execute impactful programs across the local music ecosystem

• You have a minimum of 15 years of experience across media, entertainment, or technology, with exposure to rights, partnerships, or content strategy
  • You understand how the digital music ecosystem works, including streaming models and platform dynamics
  • You know how to build and maintain senior-level relationships across complex stakeholder environments
  • You are comfortable representing an organization publicly and can communicate complex ideas in a clear, engaging way
  • You use market insights and data to guide decisions and influence strategy
  • You collaborate effectively across global teams and can balance local priorities with broader business goals
  • You communicate fluently in both Turkish and English, with confidence in formal presentations and discussions
  • You bring a strong understanding of the Turkish music landscape, including regulatory and industry dynamics
  • You care about supporting creators and contributing to a more inclusive and sustainable music ecosystem

• This role is based in Istanbul
  • We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! There will be some in person meetings, but still allows for flexibility to work from home.

The benefits available for this position include health insurance, six-month paid parental leave, 401(k) retirement plan, monthly meal allowance, 23 paid days off, paid flexible holidays, and paid sick leave. These ranges may be modified in the future.

descriptionPlain: Spotify’s Audiobooks team is building a world-class listening experience for authors, publishers, and listeners alike. As part of this team, you’ll help bring stories to life through high-quality production and thoughtful creative execution. You’ll work closely with Publishing, Partner Relations, and Content Strategy to deliver compelling audiobook content at scale while shaping how we produce and grow this space.
description: Spotify’s Audiobooks team is building a world-class listening experience for authors, publishers, and listeners alike. As part of this team, you’ll help bring stories to life through high-quality production and thoughtful creative execution. You’ll work closely with Publishing, Partner Relations, and Content Strategy to deliver compelling audiobook content at scale while shaping how we produce and grow this space.

lists.content:
• Lead and support a team of two producers, guiding day-to-day execution across multiple audiobook productions
  • Oversee end-to-end production, from pre-production, casting, recording, quality control, and delivery
  • Set clear project scopes, timelines, and deliverables, identifying risks early and keeping projects on track
  • Collaborate with internal teams and external partners to ensure alignment on creative vision and production goals
  • Act as the primary production partner to Publishing, aligning output with partner commitments and content priorities
  • Work closely with Partner Relations and Content Strategy to translate business needs into strong production plans
  • Manage budgets, contracts, and vendor relationships, ensuring smooth delivery from scoping through wrap
  • Lead casting decisions and direct recording sessions, ensuring each title meets a high creative standard
  • Build and maintain relationships with narrators and production partners to support a diverse and scalable talent network
  • Help improve and standardize production workflows to support a growing catalog of audiobook content

• You have 5+ years of experience in production, including time leading projects or teams in a media, publishing, or creative environment
  • You are experienced in managing multiple productions at once and keeping teams aligned in fast-moving environments
  • You have a strong understanding of the full audiobook production lifecycle, from planning through final delivery
  • You are comfortable working across teams and can translate business or editorial goals into clear production plans
  • You experience building and maintaining senior client relationships, instilling confidence and trust in Spotify's production capabilities.
  • You have experience working with external vendors, including defining scope, timelines, and budgets
  • You communicate clearly and build strong relationships with both creative and business partners
  • You stay organized and adaptable when priorities shift, and you’re comfortable making decisions in ambiguity
  • You care about creating inclusive, high-quality content and supporting a collaborative team environment

• This role is based in New York, NY
  • We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! There will be some in person meetings, but still allows for flexibility to work from home.

descriptionPlain:
We are seeking a Lead, User Growth Optimization to elevate our conversion optimization strategy. This role involves both implementing current optimization initiatives and developing future conversion strategies and hypotheses. The successful candidate will report to the User Growth Optimization Director within the Growth team, focusing on increasing subscriptions, reducing churn, and maximizing lifetime value.

Join the world’s fastest-growing online subscription business in a high-impact role where you will put a personal mark on the success of Spotify by delivering even faster subscriber growth.
description:
We are seeking a Lead, User Growth Optimization to elevate our conversion optimization strategy. This role involves both implementing current optimization initiatives and developing future conversion strategies and hypotheses. The successful candidate will report to the User Growth Optimization Director within the Growth team, focusing on increasing subscriptions, reducing churn, and maximizing lifetime value.

Join the world’s fastest-growing online subscription business in a high-impact role where you will put a personal mark on the success of Spotify by delivering even faster subscriber growth.

lists.content:
• Drive conversion optimization across the full user funnel, with a clear focus on subscriptions, retention, and lifetime value.
  • Develop bold, data-informed experimentation hypotheses based on quantitative analysis, user behavior, and research insights.
  • Design, build, and iterate on A/B and multivariate experiments that influence messaging, design, localization, personalization, and user journeys.
  • Implement front-end experiments using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript on Spotify’s web surfaces.
  • Work across a modern tech stack, including React and Next.js, to ensure experiments are robust, scalable, and performant.
  • Deploy and manage experiments using Spotify’s in-house experimentation platform and selected third-party testing tools.
  • Partner closely with R&D teams on implementation details and technical alignment.
  • Collaborate with marketing, growth, and business stakeholders to support campaigns, market initiatives, and specific traffic strategies.
  • Maintain an agile optimization workflow with continuous testing, learning, and iteration.

• You have 5+ years of experience working with conversion rate optimization, experimentation, and A/B testing methodologies.
  • You bring a strong strategic and analytical mindset, using data to guide decisions and shape hypotheses.
  • You are confident coding front-end experiments using JavaScript, HTML, and CSS in production environments.
  • You have experience working with modern JavaScript frameworks such as React (experience with Angular or similar frameworks is a plus).
  • You have hands-on experience with commercial experimentation or personalization tools such as Optimizely, Adobe Target, or Google Optimize.
  • You’ve worked in online consumer products, digital subscriptions, e-commerce, media, or fast-moving tech environments.
  • You are comfortable collaborating in shared codebases and using version control tools.
  • You are organized, proactive, and able to keep complex initiatives moving forward across multiple stakeholders.
  • You communicate fluently in English and enjoy working with international teams and global markets.

• This role is based in London.
  • We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! There will be some in-person meetings, but still allows for flexibility to work from home.

descriptionPlain: You’ll join Spotify’s Commerce & Subscriptions Legal team, part of the broader Products and Services Legal team, supporting a fast-moving part of the business focused on how users purchase, manage, and experience paid offerings. This team partners closely with product, engineering, design, and business stakeholders to enable innovative e-commerce experiences while managing legal risk in a thoughtful, pragmatic way.
description: You’ll join Spotify’s Commerce & Subscriptions Legal team, part of the broader Products and Services Legal team, supporting a fast-moving part of the business focused on how users purchase, manage, and experience paid offerings. This team partners closely with product, engineering, design, and business stakeholders to enable innovative e-commerce experiences while managing legal risk in a thoughtful, pragmatic way.

lists.content:
• Provide practical, commercially-focused legal advice to product managers, engineers, designers, and business partners building and scaling e-commerce products and services.
  • Support the development of new monetisation models including subscription add-ons, one-time purchases, and affiliate sales channels
  • Draft, review, and negotiate a wide range of commercial agreements with external partners
  • Advise on consumer-facing product features, including UX flows, pricing structures, and terms of service to ensure compliance with applicable laws
  • Partner with cross-functional teams exploring new ways to grow Spotify’s commerce and subscriptions offerings
  • Collaborate closely with other Legal teams to deliver consistent, joined-up legal guidance across products and services
  • Build a strong understanding of Spotify’s subscriptions business, payments systems, and customer support operations
    Contribute to scalable legal resources such as playbooks, templates, and training materials to support a growing business

• You are qualified to practice law in the UK or an EU member state with 4+ years of relevant experience working in a law firm and/or in-house legal department
  • You have experience advising on e-commerce, digital consumer transactions and/or subscription-based products
  • You're well-versed in online commerce law, including consumer protection and payments regulation and understand digital content regulations and data privacy frameworks
  • You communicate complex legal concepts clearly and effectively to non-legal audiences
  • You are comfortable working across a variety of legal topics and enjoy learning new areas of the business. You are comfortable with ambiguity and change
  • You build strong relationships and collaborate effectively across teams
  • You bring sound judgment and a pragmatic approach to balancing legal risk with business opportunity

• This role is based in London
  • We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! There will be some in person meetings, but still allows for flexibility to work from home. We ask that you come in 2-3 times per week. 
    •

descriptionPlain: You’ll join Spotify’s Legal Licensing and Business Development team, supporting partnerships that bring Spotify to listeners around the world. This team works at the intersection of technology, media, and business, helping shape how Spotify is distributed across platforms, devices, and experiences. You’ll collaborate closely with business development, finance, and licensing teams to support innovative deals that expand Spotify’s global reach.
description: You’ll join Spotify’s Legal Licensing and Business Development team, supporting partnerships that bring Spotify to listeners around the world. This team works at the intersection of technology, media, and business, helping shape how Spotify is distributed across platforms, devices, and experiences. You’ll collaborate closely with business development, finance, and licensing teams to support innovative deals that expand Spotify’s global reach.

lists.content:
• Partner with Business Development teams to support partnerships that enable Spotify distribution across platforms, devices, and services
  • Draft, review, and negotiate a wide range of commercial agreements, including technology, AI, hardware, and distribution partnerships
  • Provide clear, practical legal guidance on complex deal structures, risks, and regulatory considerations
    Collaborate with cross-functional partners across finance, accounting, and licensing to support agreement execution and implementation
  • Translate legal concepts into clear guidance that informs business strategy and decision-making
  • Help improve and scale contract templates, processes, and ways of working to support global growth
  • Support teams in navigating internal policies, approval processes, and evolving legal frameworks

• You have a law degree from an accredited university and are qualified to practice law in the UK or Sweden
  • You have 3+ years of post-qualification experience in a law firm and/or in-house legal team
  • You are experienced in drafting and negotiating commercial agreements, particularly in technology, media, AI, or IP-focused environments
  • You are comfortable working across global teams and time zones, managing multiple stakeholders and priorities
  • You communicate complex legal concepts in a clear, accessible way for non-legal partners
  • You bring a practical, solution-oriented approach to problem-solving and decision-making
  • You have experience supporting business development or partnership-driven organizations
  • You are curious about emerging technologies and understand the legal considerations around AI and digital distribution
  • You collaborate effectively and take ownership of your work while contributing to a supportive team environment

• This role is based in London or Stockholm.
  • We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! There will be some in person meetings, but still allows for flexibility to work from home.

descriptionPlain:
Spotify’s Legal team is growing. We’re facing innovative challenges that mirror the ground-breaking industry and product at the heart of our company. As we’ve expanded into more than 184 countries and our company has grown tremendously, we find ourselves facing new and interesting legal issues every day.
As Legal Counsel, Platform Liability & Enforcement at Spotify, you will help supervise Spotify’s legal takedown practices and policies and you will provide legal support for the teams that develop, implement, and support Spotify’s content policies. You will also work directly to resolve claims related to these policies and help build out processes for scale. Your experience making risk-based assessments and proactively finding solutions to complex problems will be crucial.
description:
Spotify’s Legal team is growing. We’re facing innovative challenges that mirror the ground-breaking industry and product at the heart of our company. As we’ve expanded into more than 184 countries and our company has grown tremendously, we find ourselves facing new and interesting legal issues every day.

As Legal Counsel, Platform Liability & Enforcement at Spotify, you will help supervise Spotify’s legal takedown practices and policies and you will provide legal support for the teams that develop, implement, and support Spotify’s content policies. You will also work directly to resolve claims related to these policies and help build out processes for scale. Your experience making risk-based assessments and proactively finding solutions to complex problems will be crucial.

lists.content:
• Advise on Spotify’s global compliance with platform liability frameworks and content safe harbor laws (e.g., Digital Services Act, DMCA, Section 230 of the Communications Decency Act)
  • Support managing Spotify’s legal takedown process, across content types, across all types of legal issues including intellectual property and speech-based offenses
  • Provide legal advice across Spotify on platform liability, including issue-spotting and strategizing solutions for exciting new products
  • Work with internal and external counsel and government affairs experts to track evolving legal requirements across the territories where Spotify operates
  • Counsel the teams responsible for building, managing, and implementing Spotify’s content policies, including the Trust & Safety team and the Content Protection team
  • Work on proactive enforcement efforts regarding potential abuse of the Spotify platform
  • Provide legal support for litigations and press inquiries arising out of content takedown issues
  • Work with operations teams within and outside the legal team to develop efficient, scalable practices around legal takedown requests
  • Perform rotational work as business requires

• Law degree (or equivalent)
  • 5+ years of experience working in a law firm and/or in-house legal department
  • Deep understanding of intermediary liability frameworks, with a preference for experience litigating cases involving intermediary liability
  • Proven expertise in copyright law and other content-related legal issues
  • Experience assessing and advising on a broad set of legal standards, even when those standards are new to you
  • Experience in partnering with global stakeholders
  • A passion for legal problems; you can explain legal concepts to non-lawyers
  • A problem solver who stays calm when problems come your way
  • A builder; you can work collaboratively (especially with non-lawyers) on finding practical, implementable tools and operational solutions
  • Self-motivated and can identify and complete work to be done without being instructed
  • A multi-tasker who can triage to make sure the right matters get your attention
  • You’re willing to really dig deep on projects
  • You work well as part of a team — both with other lawyers and with non-legal stakeholders

•  This role is based in New York, New York
  • We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! There will be some in person meetings, but still allows for flexibility to work from home

descriptionBody:
Spotify’s Legal team is growing. We’re facing innovative challenges that mirror the ground-breaking industry and product at the heart of our company. As we’ve expanded into more than 184 countries and our company has grown tremendously, we find ourselves facing new and interesting legal issues every day.

As Legal Counsel, Platform Liability & Enforcement at Spotify, you will help supervise Spotify’s legal takedown practices and policies and you will provide legal support for the teams that develop, implement, and support Spotify’s content policies. You will also work directly to resolve claims related to these policies and help build out processes for scale. Your experience making risk-based assessments and proactively finding solutions to complex problems will be crucial.

description:
Spotify’s Legal team is growing. We’re facing innovative challenges that mirror the ground-breaking industry and product at the heart of our company. As we’ve expanded into more than 184 countries and our company has grown tremendously, we find ourselves facing new and interesting legal issues every day.

As Legal Counsel, Platform Liability & Enforcement at Spotify, you will help supervise Spotify’s legal takedown practices and policies and you will provide legal support for the teams that develop, implement, and support Spotify’s content policies. You will also work directly to resolve claims related to these policies and help build out processes for scale. Your experience making risk-based assessments and proactively finding solutions to complex problems will be crucial.

lists.content:
• Advise on Spotify’s global compliance with platform liability frameworks and content safe harbor laws (e.g., Digital Services Act, DMCA, Section 230 of the Communications Decency Act)
  • Support managing Spotify’s legal takedown process, across content types, across all types of legal issues including intellectual property and speech-based offenses
  • Provide legal advice across Spotify on platform liability, including issue-spotting and strategizing solutions for exciting new products
  • Work with internal and external counsel and government affairs experts to track evolving legal requirements across the territories where Spotify operates
  • Counsel the teams responsible for building, managing, and implementing Spotify’s content policies, including the Trust & Safety team and the Content Protection team
  • Work on proactive enforcement efforts regarding potential abuse of the Spotify platform
  • Provide legal support for litigations and press inquiries arising out of content takedown issues
  • Work with operations teams within and outside the legal team to develop efficient, scalable practices around legal takedown requests
  • Perform rotational work as business requires

• Law degree (or equivalent)
  • 5+ years of experience working in a law firm and/or in-house legal department
  • Deep understanding of intermediary liability frameworks, with a preference for experience litigating cases involving intermediary liability
  • Proven expertise in copyright law and other content-related legal issues
  • Experience assessing and advising on a broad set of legal standards, even when those standards are new to you
  • Experience in partnering with global stakeholders
  • A passion for legal problems; you can explain legal concepts to non-lawyers
  • A problem solver who stays calm when problems come your way
  • A builder; you can work collaboratively (especially with non-lawyers) on finding practical, implementable tools and operational solutions
  • Self-motivated and can identify and complete work to be done without being instructed
  • A multi-tasker who can triage to make sure the right matters get your attention
  • You’re willing to really dig deep on projects
  • You work well as part of a team — both with other lawyers and with non-legal stakeholders

•  This role is based in London
  • We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! There will be some in person meetings, but still allows for flexibility to work from home

descriptionPlain: Spotify seeks an outstanding, highly-motivated attorney to serve as Legal Counsel, Product. As a product counsel at Spotify, you will partner with and provide guidance to our product and business teams on global, cutting-edge issues in an exciting, fast-paced environment.
description: Spotify seeks an outstanding, highly-motivated attorney to serve as Legal Counsel, Product. As a product counsel at Spotify, you will partner with and provide guidance to our product and business teams on global, cutting-edge issues in an exciting, fast-paced environment.

lists.content:
• Partner with the product and business teams at Spotify throughout all stages of product development, including ideation, design, testing and launch
  • Advise on new and innovative products, features and formats across all markets where Spotify is available, including AI systems
  • Provide legal guidance on AI-specific regulation (such as the EU AI Act) and other global regulations, including laws regarding consumer protection, privacy and data protection, content, intellectual property, online safety, minor protection, and other legal issues relevant to digital products
  • Conduct legal reviews in connection with expansion into new markets and ensure our products comply with legal developments in existing markets, including evolving standards for AI transparency and safety
  • Draft and negotiate contracts, including for third-party software and technology procurement as necessary to support the development of new features and formats
  • Establish and maintain all product-related legal documentation, such as terms of service, platform policies and consumer and creator messaging
  • Maintain a proficient understanding of international legal and policy developments that may affect Spotify’s business
  • Improve our standard form agreements and legal processes that help us scale globally with a consistent but locally-relevant approach

• You have a J.D., LL.M. or equivalent, with admission to at least one bar (or qualification for registered in-house counsel in New York or Sweden)
  • You have 5+ years of relevant experience working in a law firm and/or in-house legal department]
  • You are experienced in product counseling for technology products
  • You are excited about new technologies, in particular AI-based systems
  • You have a strong working knowledge of legal issues relating to digital products, technology, privacy, intellectual property, content and platform liability, consumer protection, media and AI
  • You have a strong track record of working with a wide range of commercial agreements
  • You are solutions oriented and self-motivated, with a willingness to dig into the details of projects
  • You are highly collaborative and work well with both legal and non-legal stakeholders
  • You take a practical, business-oriented approach to problem-solving and provide pragmatic, commercial and timely advice
  • You are comfortable with ambiguity and change
  • You have experience partnering with global stakeholders, including external counsel

• This role is based in New York, or Stockholm
  • We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! There will be some in person meetings, but still allows for flexibility to work from home. We ask that you come in 2-3 times per week.

descriptionPlain: We are seeking an experienced Legal Counsel with strong expertise in music publishing and copyright licensing to join Spotify’s Legal Licensing team. In this role, you’ll partner closely with Business Affairs, Finance, and Product teams to structure, negotiate, and draft complex licensing agreements with CMOs and music publishers. You’ll play a key role in shaping the legal frameworks that govern how musical works are used across Spotify’s service in APAC, helping enable innovation while supporting rights holders and creators.
description: We are seeking an experienced Legal Counsel with strong expertise in music publishing and copyright licensing to join Spotify’s Legal Licensing team. In this role, you’ll partner closely with Business Affairs, Finance, and Product teams to structure, negotiate, and draft complex licensing agreements with CMOs and music publishers. You’ll play a key role in shaping the legal frameworks that govern how musical works are used across Spotify’s service in APAC, helping enable innovation while supporting rights holders and creators.

lists.content:
• Provide clear, practical legal advice on music publishing, copyright, and licensing matters across APAC markets
  • Structure, draft, and negotiate agreements with music publishers and collective management organizations (CMOs)
  • Partner closely with Business Affairs and cross-functional teams to support commercial negotiations and strategic initiatives
  • Advise on legal implications of new product features, launches, and business models involving musical works
  • Help ensure compliance with contractual obligations, copyright laws, and local regulatory frameworks
  • Translate complex legal concepts into clear, actionable guidance for non-legal stakeholders
  • Contribute to scalable legal frameworks that support Spotify’s continued growth in the region

• You have a minimum of 6+ years of experience in a law firm and/or in-house legal role with exposure to music publishing or copyright law
  • You are experienced in negotiating licensing agreements involving digital services, publishers, and CMOs
  • You have a strong understanding of the commercial dynamics of the music industry and digital platforms
  • You are comfortable working across multiple jurisdictions, ideally with experience in APAC markets
  • You communicate clearly and know how to simplify complex legal topics for a range of audiences
  • You can manage multiple priorities and operate independently in a fast-paced environment
  • You care about supporting creators and rights holders in an evolving digital ecosystem
  • You bring a collaborative, inclusive mindset and align with Spotify’s values

• This role is based in Singapore or Sydney
  • We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! There will be some in person meetings, but still allows for flexibility to work from home.

descriptionPlain:
The Talk & Advertising Platform Legal Group supports Spotify's podcast business unit. As a lawyer within the group, you will partner with and provide guidance to our podcast and creator teams and support the expansion of Spotify's podcast monetization business across various markets around the world.

Our work is cutting edge, revolutionary and always evolving, so it will be up to you to continuously assess and advise the teams on legal issues using a creative and pragmatic approach.
 
You will primarily partner with and provide legal guidance to the podcast business at Spotify. You will advise colleagues on our monetization programs with podcast creators.  You will work on a range of agreements, including those with podcast publishers, hosting platforms, advertising technology partners, and third-party vendors. You will also provide product guidance on the podcast monetization platforms in order to ensure compliance with regulatory requirements as Spotify's podcast business expands.
description:
The Talk & Advertising Platform Legal Group supports Spotify's podcast business unit. As a lawyer within the group, you will partner with and provide guidance to our podcast and creator teams and support the expansion of Spotify's podcast monetization business across various markets around the world.

Our work is cutting edge, revolutionary and always evolving, so it will be up to you to continuously assess and advise the teams on legal issues using a creative and pragmatic approach.
 
You will primarily partner with and provide legal guidance to the podcast business at Spotify. You will advise colleagues on our monetization programs with podcast creators.  You will work on a range of agreements, including those with podcast publishers, hosting platforms, advertising technology partners, and third-party vendors. You will also provide product guidance on the podcast monetization platforms in order to ensure compliance with regulatory requirements as Spotify's podcast business expands.

lists.content:
• Partner with our podcast and creator monetization teams to understand their priorities and help them achieve their objectives.
  • Support automated and programmatic monetization channel workstreams across Spotify's podcast platforms.
  • Manage drafting, review, and negotiation of license agreements and monetization program terms across a range of commercial arrangements with external partners, and collaborate with key internal stakeholders to bring those agreements to execution.
  • Synthesize regulatory issues and legal implications that may impact Spotify's evolving podcast monetization technology stack and provide compliance guidance to business partners and others within the legal team.
  • Provide effective and commercially focused product counseling in relation to new podcast monetization features, creator tools, and UX copy.
  • Work closely with other key stakeholders in the Finance, Product, and Engineering teams to provide holistic support to Spotify's podcast monetization business.

• You are admitted to practice law in California or qualified to act as Registered In-House Counsel in California. You have at least 5+ years of post-qualification experience working in a law firm and/or in-house legal department. Existing in-house experience is preferable but not essential.
  • You have experience working in the podcast, digital audio, or broader media ecosystem — as external counsel advising creators, publishers, and technology platforms; and/or in the legal department of a large, scaled business with a focus on podcasters, media creators, or media monetization matters.
  • You have a solid understanding of the podcast landscape, including how podcast advertising, host-read sponsorships, programmatic audio, and creator monetization programs operate, as well as the license agreements and monetization structures that support them.
  • Strong working knowledge of and experience with product counseling, intellectual property, and legal issues relating to digital audio, podcast distribution, programmatic advertising, privacy, consumer protection, and media. Experience in a consumer-facing technology or media organization with a significant podcast or digital audio business is a plus.
  • You consistently deliver clear, concise, practical, and pragmatic advice, with a proven ability to make risk-based decisions and to develop innovative and business-focused solutions to commercial challenges and regulatory developments.
  • You are comfortable balancing a varied body of work, moving at pace, and operating in areas where there is no perfect answer and ambiguity is high. You have a demonstrated willingness to dig into the details of projects and work well as part of a team — both with other lawyers and with non-legal stakeholders.
  • You have excellent interpersonal skills with an absolute commitment to professionalism and collegiality.
  • Fluency in English is essential; other languages are a plus.

• This role is based in Los Angeles, CA.
  • We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! There will be some in person meetings, but still allows for flexibility to work from home. We ask that you come in 2-3 times per week.

descriptionPlain:
The Personalization team makes deciding what to play next easier and more enjoyable for every listener. From Blend to Discover Weekly, we’re behind some of Spotify’s most-loved features. We built them by understanding the world of music and podcasts better than anyone else. Join us and you’ll keep millions of users listening by making great recommendations to each and every one of them. 
 
We are looking for a Machine Learning Engineer to join the Personalization (PZN) team - an area of hardworking engineers that are passionate about understanding what drives user satisfaction with Spotify - and who make impactful changes to Home recommendation systems to achieve this goal. As an integral part of the squad, you will collaborate with research scientists, data scientists and other engineers across PZN in prototyping and productizing state-of-the-art ML at the intersection of recommendations and long-term user satisfaction.
description:
The Personalization team makes deciding what to play next easier and more enjoyable for every listener. From Blend to Discover Weekly, we’re behind some of Spotify’s most-loved features. We built them by understanding the world of music and podcasts better than anyone else. Join us and you’ll keep millions of users listening by making great recommendations to each and every one of them. 
 
We are looking for a Machine Learning Engineer to join the Personalization (PZN) team - an area of hardworking engineers that are passionate about understanding what drives user satisfaction with Spotify - and who make impactful changes to Home recommendation systems to achieve this goal. As an integral part of the squad, you will collaborate with research scientists, data scientists and other engineers across PZN in prototyping and productizing state-of-the-art ML at the intersection of recommendations and long-term user satisfaction.

lists.content:
• Contribute to designing, scaling/building, evaluating, integrating, shipping, and refining reward signals for recommendations by hands-on ML development.
  • Lead collaborations and align across Personalization to integrate and A/B test mid-term signals in various recommendation systems.
  • Promote and role-model best practices of ML systems development, testing, evaluation, etc., both inside the team as well as throughout the organization.
    •

• You have a strong background in machine learning, enjoy applying theory to develop real-world applications, with expertise in statistics and optimization, especially in sequential models, transformers, generative AI and large language models, and relevant fine-tuning processes.
  • You have hands-on experience with large cross-collaborative machine learning projects and managing stakeholders.
  • You have hands-on experience implementing production machine learning systems at scale in Java, Scala, Python, or similar languages.
  • Experience with PyTorch, Ray, Hugging Face and related tools is required.
  • You have some experience with large scale, distributed data processing frameworks/tools like Apache Beam, Apache Spark, or even our open source API for it - Scio, and cloud platforms like GCP or AWS.
  • You care about agile software processes, data-driven development, reliability, and disciplined experimentation.
    •

• We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! For this role, you can be within the North America region as long as we have a work location.
  • This team operates within the Eastern Standard time zone for collaboration.
    •
