categories.team: Advertising Sales - Emerging and Scaled Sales

descriptionPlain:
Sell what you love. For us and millions of users across the globe, that’s Spotify. Join the Sales team and you’ll build the relationships that help grow our business in existing markets and beyond. We don’t just sell creative solutions to our clients and partners, we help to shape them; using our expert knowledge of ad products, sales channels and the industry to impact the way the world experiences music and podcasts. 


We focus on building trust in Spotify’s advertising platform. This team sits at the center of Product, Engineering, Sales, and business teams, ensuring that our ads ecosystem runs smoothly and delivers a reliable experience for advertisers and listeners alike. As Platform Integrity Lead, you’ll play a key role in protecting revenue, strengthening client relationships, and improving how we respond to and learn from incidents across the Ads business.
description:
Sell what you love. For us and millions of users across the globe, that’s Spotify. Join the Sales team and you’ll build the relationships that help grow our business in existing markets and beyond. We don’t just sell creative solutions to our clients and partners, we help to shape them; using our expert knowledge of ad products, sales channels and the industry to impact the way the world experiences music and podcasts. 

We focus on building trust in Spotify’s advertising platform. This team sits at the center of Product, Engineering, Sales, and business teams, ensuring that our ads ecosystem runs smoothly and delivers a reliable experience for advertisers and listeners alike. As Platform Integrity Lead, you’ll play a key role in protecting revenue, strengthening client relationships, and improving how we respond to and learn from incidents across the Ads business.

lists.content:
• Own and evolve the cross-functional incident management process for Spotify’s Ads business, ensuring fast, coordinated, and effective resolution
  • Act as the central point of coordination across Ads R&D, Sales, Advertiser Solutions, Business Operations, Finance, Legal, and Marketing
  • Partner with technical and operational teams to monitor incoming ad serving issues and identify those with meaningful business impact
  • Build and improve structured bug intake and ticketing workflows to increase clarity, speed, and accountability across the incident lifecycle
  • Identify trends and recurring issues, and partner with Ads R&D to drive long-term solutions
  • Lead internal communication strategies during incidents, ensuring stakeholders have clear, timely, and accurate updates
  • Collaborate with cross-functional partners to support revenue teams in mitigating revenue loss and maintaining strong client experiences
  • Make informed escalation decisions based on incident severity and business impact, ensuring the right level of visibility
  • Deliver clear reporting on revenue impact and incident trends to Finance and leadership teams

• You have 7+ years of experience in ad operations, product operations, sales systems, or a related role within a digital advertising environment
  • You bring 3+ years of experience working with programmatic advertising or technical ad systems
  • You understand ad serving, measurement, billing, and order management systems, and how issues in these areas affect revenue and customer trust
  • You have experience building and improving operational processes in cross-functional, fast-moving environments
  • You are comfortable navigating ambiguity and making decisions that balance technical constraints with business priorities
  • You communicate clearly and confidently, and can align stakeholders across different teams and levels
  • You use data to inform decisions and can translate complex information into clear actions
  • You have experience with incident management processes and tools such as Jira, CRM systems, or reporting platforms
• We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! For this role, you can be within the Eastern Standard time zone.

description:
Sell what you love. For us and millions of users across the globe, that’s Spotify. Join the Sales team and you’ll build the relationships that help grow our business in existing markets and beyond. We don’t just sell creative solutions to our clients and partners, we help to shape them; using our expert knowledge of ad products, sales channels and the industry to impact the way the world experiences music and podcasts. 

We’re looking for an outstanding Podcast Client Partner who operates as a sales content specialist, to join our Podcast Specialist Team - the bridge between Spotify's top podcasts and our sales organization. You have a proven record of revenue generation and content sales, a genuine passion for podcasts, and a gift for communicating their value to clients and partners. Most importantly, your work will shape the way the world experiences audio.

lists.content:
• Work with Spotify Studios content teams to create unique and compelling integrated sponsorship opportunities for clients.  You will spend time with our podcast hosts to understand their passion points, what brands they love and how to create more revenue opportunities within their content.
  • Create strategic, GTM plans for new shows and ideate on how advertisers can partner with us at launch. 
  • Lead client and agency presentations showcasing how our integrated ideas come to life.
  • Partner closely with the Spotify frontline sales team to tailor ideas for specific verticals and clients.  
  • Partner internally with sales support, marketing and Spotify Studios teams to ensure our ideas are operationalized.
  • Focus on brand sell, educating and inspiring our clients to leverage Spotify podcast content to enhance their business goals and outcomes.
  • Meet and exceed revenue expectations attached to specific podcasts.
  • Attend weekly and monthly NA Sales Meetings to showcase ideas that will help create enthusiasm with clients. 
  • Represent Spotify at specific events and serve as a brand ambassador.
  • Manage activity using salesforce.com and other internal systems.

• You have a Bachelor’s degree.
  • You have 5+ years of experience in a media agency/online sales role; podcast, and/or content sales preferred.
  • You are passionate about podcasts and have a deep understanding of our hosts and slate of shows.
  • You have knowledge of online and offline media and have the ability to be creative with audio and digital client solutions.
  • You have strong negotiation skills and the ability to present effectively and close the deal.
  • You have the ability to build a pipeline and a proven track record of exceeding goals.
  • You have a deep understanding of the digital ecosystem.
  • You are a team player, smart, adapt well with change and have a positive attitude.
  • You are able to work in a fast-paced, exciting environment and across external and internal teams to deliver creative, fresh campaigns.

• This position will be based in New York City.
  • We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! There will be some in person meetings, but still allows for flexibility to work from home. We ask that you come in 2-3 times per week.

descriptionPlain:
Spotify’s mission is to unlock the potential of human creativity by giving a million creative artists the opportunity to live off their art and billions of fans the chance to enjoy and be passionate about these creators.
 
The Subscriptions Mission builds and evolves Spotify’s subscription products and marketplace experiences to drive sustainable user and revenue growth globally. We focus on awareness, acquisition, activation, retention, and monetization strategies that help users unlock the full value of Spotify while enabling the business to scale efficiently and responsibly.
 
User Understanding sits within Subscriptions and focuses on building the intelligence layer that makes our growth efforts smarter—through decisioning, data signals, and ML models that power personalization across surfaces and lifecycle moments. You’ll work at the intersection of machine learning innovation and commercial strategy, partnering closely with ML engineers, data scientists, data platform teams, product insights, and business stakeholders to shape high-impact growth initiatives.
description:
Spotify’s mission is to unlock the potential of human creativity by giving a million creative artists the opportunity to live off their art and billions of fans the chance to enjoy and be passionate about these creators.
 
The Subscriptions Mission builds and evolves Spotify’s subscription products and marketplace experiences to drive sustainable user and revenue growth globally. We focus on awareness, acquisition, activation, retention, and monetization strategies that help users unlock the full value of Spotify while enabling the business to scale efficiently and responsibly.
 
User Understanding sits within Subscriptions and focuses on building the intelligence layer that makes our growth efforts smarter—through decisioning, data signals, and ML models that power personalization across surfaces and lifecycle moments. You’ll work at the intersection of machine learning innovation and commercial strategy, partnering closely with ML engineers, data scientists, data platform teams, product insights, and business stakeholders to shape high-impact growth initiatives.

lists.content:
• Set the product vision and multi-quarter strategy for ML- and AI-driven subscriber growth across awareness, acquisition, activation, retention, and monetization. These strategies will define and inform how we develop data signal sourcing, data platform capabilities, and model validation.
  • Own a portfolio of initiatives end-to-end, aligning multiple squads and stakeholders around clear goals, sequencing, and measurable outcomes (conversion, churn, ARPU, LTV).
  • Turn behavioral and revenue signals into crisp hypotheses, define success metrics and guardrails, and drive an experimentation roadmap that’s prioritized by impact and learning value.
  • Establish strong measurement practices (A/B testing, incrementality, causal inference where appropriate), and ensure we scale only what demonstrates durable lift against strong baselines.
  • Set direction to prioritize and develop data signals, attributes, data products, and feature store requirements
  • Drive lifecycle and personalization strategies (including trial design, offer targeting, win-back, and plan nudges) that balance user value, revenue outcomes, and long-term trust.
  • Partner with pricing and packaging stakeholders to design experiments that improve monetization while remaining transparent, locally relevant, and sustainable.
  • Make pragmatic decisions about when to use ML, GenAI, heuristics, or rules, balancing ROI, complexity, latency, maintainability, and launch risk. Start with simple solutions before investing in complex systems.
  • Ensure production readiness for ML-backed experiences through monitoring, performance evaluation, guardrails, data quality standards, and rollout plans that work at global scale.
  • Communicate trade-offs and insights clearly to senior leadership, translating technical complexity into business impact and crisp decisions.
  • Elevate product craft across the mission by mentoring other PMs, strengthening decision frameworks, and building a culture of learning, accountability, and inclusive collaboration.

• You bring 8+ years of product management experience, including leading data-driven or ML-backed products from discovery through global rollout.
  • You have led platform or shared-capability product work where driving alignment and adoption was key to success, not just shipping features
  • You have strong technical fluency in experimentation design, A/B testing, causal inference concepts, ML fundamentals, recommender/personalization systems, and data infrastructure (feature stores, attribute systems, data pipelines, data governance)..
  • You have experience defining and governing shared data products or data layers - establishing standards and processes
  • You consistently connect product decisions to measurable business outcomes, with a track record of improving conversion, retention, churn, ARPU, and/or LTV.
  • You have experience in subscription or e-commerce business models, including lifecycle optimization, offers, pricing, packaging, or marketplace dynamics.
  • You demonstrate strong judgment in choosing the simplest effective solution, and you know when advanced ML or GenAI is worth the trade-offs. You resist over-engineering and insist on proving incremental value.
  • You build responsibly, considering fairness, bias mitigation, privacy, and localization — especially when personalization affects pricing, offers, or eligibility.
  • You lead with clarity and empathy, give and receive feedback well, and raise the bar through coaching and principled decision-making.
  • You influence without authority across engineering, data science, design, analytics, finance, and commercial stakeholders, and you’re skilled at aligning teams through ambiguity. You are experienced with resolving trade-offs between competing priorities.

• This role is based in London, United Kingdom or Stockholm, Sweden.
  • We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! There will be some in-person meetings, but still allows for flexibility to work from home.

descriptionPlain:
We design Spotify’s consumer experience—end to end, moment to moment, across every screen, platform, and partner integration. Our mission is to make listening feel effortless, personal, and joyful for billions of users around the world. That means turning complexity into clarity across hundreds of touchpoints—from our mobile and desktop apps to the smart speakers, TVs, cars, and integrations where Spotify shows up every day. If it touches a consumer, we shape it. We bring deep insight into human behavior, design, and technology to craft experiences that feel intuitive, expressive, and unmistakably Spotify.
We’re seeking a Principal Product Manager to join our consumer experience team.  At Spotify, helping users decide what to play next is at the heart of the experience. This role focuses on shaping how people explore music, podcasts, and more—through intuitive, personalized, and engaging discovery experiences across the app.
As a Principal Product Management, you’ll work on some of Spotify’s most visible surfaces, connecting user intent, recommendations, and content into a seamless journey that helps millions of listeners find their next favorite thing

This role focuses on shaping how people explore music, podcasts, and more—through intuitive, personalized, and engaging discovery experiences across the app.

As a Principal Product Management, you’ll work on some of Spotify’s most visible surfaces, connecting user intent, recommendations, and content into a seamless journey that helps millions of listeners find their next favorite thing

lists.content:
• Define and drive the long-term product vision for how users explore and decide what to play on Spotify

  • Shape discovery experiences across key consumer surfaces such as Home, Browse, and Search

  • Lead cross-surface strategy that connects personalized recommendations, editorial context, and user intent into a cohesive journey

  • Partner with engineering, design, and data science to evolve scalable discovery platforms and components used across Spotify

  • Identify opportunities to reduce decision friction and make exploration feel effortless and engaging

  • Influence roadmap and priorities across multiple teams, ensuring alignment with broader consumer experience strategy

  • Use experimentation, behavioral data, & user insights to inform strategy & continuously improve outcomes

  • Act as a senior product leader, mentoring others and raising the bar for product thinking, clarity, and impact

• You have 10+ years of product management experience working on consumer-facing products

  • You have experience owning or influencing large product areas that span multiple teams or surfaces

  • You have a deep understanding of discovery, search, or personalization systems

  • You know how to turn complex systems into simple, intuitive user experiences

  • You are comfortable leading through influence in highly cross-functional environments

  • You care deeply about product craft and building high-quality user experiences at scale

  • You use data, experimentation, and judgment to guide decisions and strategy

  • You are a strong collaborator who builds alignment across engineering, design, and data partners

• This role is based in New York, NY.

  • We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! There will be some in person meetings, but still allows for flexibility to work from home.

The United States base range for this position is $119,424.00 - 170,606.00, plus equity. The benefits available for this position include health insurance, six month paid parental leave, 401(k) retirement plan, monthly meal allowance, 23 paid days off, 13 paid flexible holidays. This range encompasses multiple levels. Leveling is determined during the interview process. Placement in a level depends on relevant work history and interview performance. Spotify is an equal opportunity employer. You are welcome at Spotify for who you are, no matter where you come from, what you look like, or what’s playing in your headphones. Our platform is for everyone, and so is our workplace. The more voices we have represented and amplified in our business, the more we will all thrive, contribute, and be forward-thinking! So bring us your personal experience, your perspectives, and your background. It’s in our differences that we will find the power to keep revolutionizing the way the world listens.
 
At Spotify, we are passionate about inclusivity and making sure our entire recruitment process is accessible to everyone. We have ways to request reasonable accommodations during the interview process and help assist in what you need. If you need accommodations at any stage of the application or interview process, please let us know - we’re here to support you in any way we can.

descriptionPlain:
Our mission on the Advertising Product & Technology team is to build a next generation advertising platform that aligns with our unique value proposition for audio and video. We work to scale the user experience for hundreds of millions of fans and hundreds of thousands of advertisers. This scale brings unique challenges as well as tremendous opportunities for our artists and creators.


Spotify's mission is to unlock the potential of human creativity — giving a million artists the opportunity to live off their art, and billions of fans the chance to be inspired by it. Spotify Advertising supports this mission by helping brands connect with listeners through meaningful, effective ad experiences.

This is a pivotal moment for Spotify Advertising. As we scale globally and evolve into a performance-focused, AI-powered platform, design plays a central role in simplifying complex systems, building trust with advertisers, and driving scalable product growth. Our long-term vision is an intuitive platform embedded in advertiser workflows, powered by automation, measurement, and operational excellence.
 
We're looking for a mid-level product designer to join our Advertising R&D team. You'll own design quality and cohesion across a set of features in Spotify's advertiser-facing tools, working on high-priority areas including campaign creation, audience targeting, reporting, optimization, creative tooling, measurement, and internal platforms. If you enjoy solving complex problems alongside cross-functional partners and are comfortable navigating ambiguous, technical domains (including AI, automation, and experimentation), we'd love to hear from you.
description:
Our mission on the Advertising Product & Technology team is to build a next generation advertising platform that aligns with our unique value proposition for audio and video. We work to scale the user experience for hundreds of millions of fans and hundreds of thousands of advertisers. This scale brings unique challenges as well as tremendous opportunities for our artists and creators.

Spotify's mission is to unlock the potential of human creativity — giving a million artists the opportunity to live off their art, and billions of fans the chance to be inspired by it. Spotify Advertising supports this mission by helping brands connect with listeners through meaningful, effective ad experiences.

This is a pivotal moment for Spotify Advertising. As we scale globally and evolve into a performance-focused, AI-powered platform, design plays a central role in simplifying complex systems, building trust with advertisers, and driving scalable product growth. Our long-term vision is an intuitive platform embedded in advertiser workflows, powered by automation, measurement, and operational excellence.
 
We're looking for a mid-level product designer to join our Advertising R&D team. You'll own design quality and cohesion across a set of features in Spotify's advertiser-facing tools, working on high-priority areas including campaign creation, audience targeting, reporting, optimization, creative tooling, measurement, and internal platforms. If you enjoy solving complex problems alongside cross-functional partners and are comfortable navigating ambiguous, technical domains (including AI, automation, and experimentation), we'd love to hear from you.

lists.content:
• Design end-to-end user experiences across Spotify's self-serve advertising platform (Ads Manager), contributing to features like ad creation, targeting, reporting, optimization, and measurement.
  • Work within and contribute to a shared design system used across Ads Manager and internal tools, with attention to component reusability, accessibility, and visual consistency.
  • Collaborate with engineers to translate backend systems, APIs, and ML-powered features into interfaces that are intuitive and performant.
  • Partner with PMs, engineers, data scientists, researchers, and content designers to shape product direction, advocate for user needs, and help influence your team's roadmap.
  • Build prototypes using real data and motion where relevant to explore technical constraints, validate interaction models, and communicate product behavior in dynamic, data-driven environments.
  • Work alongside researchers and data scientists to identify the right questions to answer, and use insights to deepen your team's understanding of user problems.
  • Support design efforts on internal tools used by operational teams, helping reduce manual processes and improve the efficiency and reliability of internal workflows.
  • Contribute to a feedback-rich team culture that values critique, iteration, and craft excellence.

• You have experience designing complex, data-intensive B2B digital products. We typically look for around 3+ years of experience, though we value the depth and quality of your work over years alone.
  • You take ownership of design quality across your squad and can manage priorities effectively when working across multiple features or workstreams.
  • You have a strong visual design foundation and care about the details that elevate work from functional to excellent.
  • You connect design decisions to business goals and customer needs, and can advocate for those decisions clearly with stakeholders.
  • You're curious about AI, whether that's using AI tools in your design workflow, designing AI-powered features, or both.
  • You're comfortable with ambiguity and bring structure and curiosity to problems where there isn't always a clear path forward.
  • You actively seek feedback on your work and thinking, and give thoughtful critique in return.
  • You foster inclusive collaboration: you proactively bring others in and hold space for different perspectives.
  • Nice to have: Experience designing advertising, monetization, or creative automation tools.
  • Nice to have: Background in operational tooling or designing for internal users with complex, high-volume workflows.
  • Nice to have: Experience contributing to or maintaining large-scale design systems.

• We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! For this role, it can be within the North America region in which we have a work location.
  • This team collaborates across the Eastern time zone

Together with product managers, engineers, researchers, and content designers, you’ll help shape seamless and adaptive experiences across integrations that reach millions of users globally.

lists.content:
• Build thoughtful, accessible experiences across AI assistants, chat interfaces, and connected devices.
  • Use AI-assisted tools and in-build prototyping to iterate fast while maintaining design quality and clarity.
  • Collaborate with localization teams and regional partners  to ensure experiences feel relevant and intuitive.
  • Work closely with Content Designers (Prompt / Conversational), User Research, Product, and Engineering to deliver cohesive outcomes.
  • Apply and extend Spotify’s build language to new partner contexts while maintaining craft consistency.
  • Use research findings to advise decisions and continually improve accessibility and happiness in every experience.

• Experience in Product Design experience across platforms, integrations, or global products.
  • Competent in scaling partner agnostic foundations into partner surfaces and contexts.
  • Skilled in Figma and prototyping tools; eager to explore AI-enabled workflows.
  • Collaborative and curious, motivated by working with cross-cultural teams and external partners.
  • Comfortable balancing speed, structure, and creativity in a fast-moving environment.
  • It's a plus if you have experience crafting for APAC markets or AI-adjacent interfaces (assistants, messaging apps, OEM devices).

• This role is based in London or  Stockholm.
  • We offer the flexibility to work where you work best. Some in-person time helps us connect and create together. This position will require travel across the US, Europe and Asia.

This team works at the intersection of design, external partnerships, and emerging interaction models to bring Spotify to listeners, wherever they are. Together with product managers, engineers, researchers, and content designers, you’ll help shape seamless and adaptive experiences across integrations that reach millions of users globally.

descriptionPlain:
We design Spotify’s consumer experience—end to end, moment to moment, across every screen, platform, and partner integration. Our mission is to make listening feel effortless, personal, and joyful for users around the world. That means turning complexity into clarity across hundreds of touchpoints—from our mobile and desktop apps to the smart speakers, TVs, cars, and integrations where Spotify shows up every day. If it touches a consumer, we shape it. We bring deep insight into human behavior, design, and technology to craft experiences that feel intuitive, expressive, and unmistakably Spotify.
 
We’re looking for mid-level to senior product designers with strong visual design skills to join the team focusing on the core mobile experience. This team works on some of the most essential parts of the Spotify experience that shapes some of Spotify’s most iconic and emotionally resonant experiences - the features that help people connect with music, organize their libraries, and engage more deeply with what they love. You might be designing how users share and discover music with friends, how they create customise and manage playlists, or Whether it’s evolving how users organize their music, creating new ways to share audio socially, or integrating AI to help users find their next favorite song, you will work on the products that define the Core Spotify experience.
 
It’s a high-impact, fast-moving space where priorities evolve as we explore new ways to bring users closer to the content they care about. As a designer here, you’ll have the opportunity to shape foundational experiences that millions of people use every day.This is a role for a designer who thrives on variety, moves quickly, and wants to see their work impact millions of listeners across multiple surfaces

It’s a high-impact, fast-moving space where priorities evolve as we explore new ways to bring users closer to the content they care about. As a designer here, you’ll have the opportunity to shape foundational experiences that millions of people use every day.This is a role for a designer who thrives on variety, moves quickly, and wants to see their work impact millions of listeners across multiple surfaces

lists.content:
• Contribute to the design quality of high-priority features across the teams portfolio, moving between projects as product strategies evolve.
  • Apply Spotify's design principles to generate thoughtful, user-centered solutions grounded in clear problem statements, constraints, and team goals.
  • Design across core Spotify surfaces that drive long-term engagement, retention, and user satisfaction.
  • Work on a range of experiences—from social sharing to playlist curation and library organization—adapting as priorities evolve.
  • Frame design problems clearly by defining user needs, success criteria, constraints, and tradeoffs.
  • Explore multiple concepts and directions, knowing when to go deep and when to pivot based on insights and feedback.
  • Collaborate closely with product, engineering, research, and data partners to bring ideas to life.
  • Leverage AI-powered tools to explore ideas, synthesize insights, prototype, and improve design efficiency and quality.
  • Take ownership of the end-to-end design process, from early exploration through to final execution and iteration.
  • Contribute to the broader design community by sharing work, feedback, and learnings.

• You have experience designing mobile consumer products and can contribute independently to end-to-end design work.
  • You know how to craft thoughtful interaction design, information architecture, and user journeys.
  • You are comfortable working across ambiguity and can balance multiple product areas or shifting priorities.
  • You care about understanding user needs and grounding your decisions in research and data.
  • You have experience designing for mobile platforms and understand iOS and Android conventions.
  • You are curious about personalization, AI, and how intelligent systems shape user experiences and trust.
  • You communicate your design decisions clearly and can connect user needs to product outcomes.
  • You enjoy collaborating with cross-functional partners and actively seek feedback to improve your work.
  • You are comfortable using AI-enabled tools as part of your workflow.
  • You have a portfolio that demonstrates your design process, thinking, and impact.

• This role is based in London or Stockholm.
  • We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! There will be some in person meetings, but still allows for flexibility to work from home.

text: Product Designer - Core Experience (Core Value & Engagement)

descriptionPlain:
Do you love live events? Do you enjoy solving new and unique problems? Are you comfortable working on early-stage products at scale? The Live Music team is on a mission to build the biggest, most trusted place for live events, and we’re looking for a Product Designer to help shape the future of live event discovery.
 
Our Product & Design team is focused on defining and delivering the best experiences possible for live music fans and helping artists succeed in a live context. We’re doing this via new in-app experiences to help fans seamlessly learn about live events, tools for artists to engage with their fans, and internal systems for our teams to help maintain content quality. We’re a small team with big ambitions and unique problems to solve, and we’re looking for a Product Designer to join our team to help define and execute features in all three of these areas.

Our Product & Design team is focused on defining and delivering the best experiences possible for live music fans and helping artists succeed in a live context. We’re doing this via new in-app experiences to help fans seamlessly learn about live events, tools for artists to engage with their fans, and internal systems for our teams to help maintain content quality. We’re a small team with big ambitions and unique problems to solve, and we’re looking for a Product Designer to join our team to help define and execute features in all three of these areas.

lists.content:
• Work on projects across the Spotify’s Live Music products — in-app surfaces, internal systems, artist tooling
  • Support product development by utilizing your design craft, through concept sketches, storyboards, product mock-ups, high-fidelity prototypes
  • Think big, but start small — identify both new opportunities and also the first step to exploring them
  • Work in a variety of modes, from broad explorative prompts to tightly defined launch cycles
  • Partner closely with product managers, engineers, data scientists and researchers to uncover problems and define solutions
  • Design with focus on inclusivity and accessibility in order to reach users worldwide
  • Collaborate with other design teams across Spotify working on app experiences and artist tools

• You are an established Product Designer with experience owning and driving complex projects
  • You have experience designing for either business or consumer-focused products at scale
  • You are comfortable collaborating directly with engineers and product managers to define and deliver solutions
  • You enjoy crafting in complex and sometimes ambiguous spaces where there is no “paved path”
  • You care about design craft and the details that elevate work from good to great
  • You understand and can articulate how your work intersects with or augments existing design systems
  • You believe in creating a culture of feedback and actively seek out feedback on your work and approaches
  • You have 3+ years of relevant design experience

• This role is based in London
  • We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! There will be some in-person meetings, but give you the flexibility to work from home

descriptionPlain:
Mission Statement
The Platform team creates the technology that enables Spotify to learn quickly and scale easily, enabling rapid growth in our users and our business around the globe. Spanning many disciplines, we work to make the business work; creating the infrastructure, tooling, frameworks, and capabilities needed to welcome a billion customers.
About the Team
You’ll join a team building shared web tooling, frameworks, and operator-facing products that power Spotify’s internal financial workflows. Our mission is simple (but not easy): make complex work easier, smoother, and more intuitive for our operators, while keeping our infrastructure scalable and easy to build on.
We focus on creating a consistent, thoughtful experience across internal tools and connected SaaS systems, helping finance operators move seamlessly between products every day. Alongside mature platforms, we’re also building new workflow and task management experiences, with a growing investment in AI-powered capabilities to support smarter decision-making.
description:
Mission Statement

The Platform team creates the technology that enables Spotify to learn quickly and scale easily, enabling rapid growth in our users and our business around the globe. Spanning many disciplines, we work to make the business work; creating the infrastructure, tooling, frameworks, and capabilities needed to welcome a billion customers.

About the Team

You’ll join a team building shared web tooling, frameworks, and operator-facing products that power Spotify’s internal financial workflows. Our mission is simple (but not easy): make complex work easier, smoother, and more intuitive for our operators, while keeping our infrastructure scalable and easy to build on.

We focus on creating a consistent, thoughtful experience across internal tools and connected SaaS systems, helping finance operators move seamlessly between products every day. Alongside mature platforms, we’re also building new workflow and task management experiences, with a growing investment in AI-powered capabilities to support smarter decision-making.

lists.content:
• Contribute to the product vision and roadmap for shared web tooling and operator-facing products used by finance teams
  • Support and evolve a portfolio of internal platforms and workflow systems, from established tools to new capabilities
  • Work closely with R&D and end users to understand needs, validate problems, and deliver meaningful solutions
  • Lead product discovery for new features and improvements, focusing on usability, clarity, and operator efficiency
  • Help shape AI-assisted capabilities within workflows, ensuring they are useful, transparent, and trustworthy
  • Partner with engineers to define shared web frameworks and components that enable other teams to build efficiently while maintaining UX consistency
  • Balance short-term delivery with long-term platform thinking, including scalability, reliability, and maintainability
  • Communicate clearly with stakeholders to align on priorities, trade-offs, and outcomes
  • Collaborate closely with engineering and design to support a healthy, high-performing team environment

• You enjoy collaborating with cross-functional partners and driving outcomes through strong relationships
  • You are experienced with, or curious about, building operator-facing or workflow-driven products and simplifying complex systems
  • You are comfortable working closely with engineers and can engage in technical discussions around system design and architecture
  • You have a solid understanding of modern AI/ML concepts such as large language models, prompt design, evaluation, and their trade-offs
  • You understand how AI can support human decision-making while maintaining transparency and trust
  • You learn quickly and synthesize inputs from users, data, and technical contexts to inform decisions
  • You communicate clearly and adapt your storytelling to different audiences
  • You care deeply about user-centered, data-informed product development
  • You are interested in platforms and shared tooling, and how they enable teams while maintaining a strong user experience
  • You have experience with internal tools, financial systems, or workflow-based products, or you’re excited to learn in this space
  • You are comfortable navigating ambiguity and influencing decisions through insight

• This role is based in Stockholm
  • We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! There will be some in person meetings, but still allows for flexibility to work from home

descriptionPlain:
We are seeking a Product Marketing Manager to join the Global Monetization Product Marketing team. The Monetization Product Marketing team partners with Spotify’s R&D teams to build innovative new ad solutions by defining market requirements and developing go-to-market strategies to increase product adoption and revenue growth. 
In this role, you will have the opportunity to make a significant impact on our rapidly growing ads business– as you help shape the strategy for Spotify’s advertising upper-funnel measurement solutions. You will be joining a fast-paced environment where you interface daily with cross-functional experts to launch and grow solutions that help our customers understand which strategies are helping them reach their goals. 
You will tap into creative, analytical, and tactical strengths – to effectively bring product to market, and the voice of the market back to product. You’ll grow as a person and leader in this strategic pillar of our ads business, all the while helping advertisers better understand the impact of their marketing decisions.
description:
We are seeking a Product Marketing Manager to join the Global Monetization Product Marketing team. The Monetization Product Marketing team partners with Spotify’s R&D teams to build innovative new ad solutions by defining market requirements and developing go-to-market strategies to increase product adoption and revenue growth. 

In this role, you will have the opportunity to make a significant impact on our rapidly growing ads business– as you help shape the strategy for Spotify’s advertising upper-funnel measurement solutions. You will be joining a fast-paced environment where you interface daily with cross-functional experts to launch and grow solutions that help our customers understand which strategies are helping them reach their goals. 

You will tap into creative, analytical, and tactical strengths – to effectively bring product to market, and the voice of the market back to product. You’ll grow as a person and leader in this strategic pillar of our ads business, all the while helping advertisers better understand the impact of their marketing decisions.

lists.content:
• Drive product and feature development of Spotify’s advertising measurement solutions, tapping into your market knowledge to help R&D teams identify and prioritize the most impactful work.
  • Deeply understand customer segments and collect high-quality product feedback to influence product roadmaps and to guide global marketing tactics.
  • Develop product positioning strategies that clearly articulate the value proposition of Spotify and differentiate us in market.
  • Lead product launches with a multitude of cross-functional teams including sales, operations, PR, marketing, and more. 
  • Support global product adoption and growth through market expansion, sales enablement, and thought leadership.

• You have a minimum of 5+ years of experience within the digital advertising/programmatic space, with a keen understanding of advertising measurement and both 1P and 3P upper-funnel solutions, especially brand lift and audience/media verification. Product marketing experience preferred.
  • You have a BA/BS degree or equivalent; master’s degree a plus.
  • You have a strong experience leading cross-functional teams and a track record of influencing senior stakeholders in product/engineering, partnerships, operations and sales.
  • You have experience with customer research and are proficient using data/insights to inform product/GTM strategy
  • You have strong opinions about what we should build and why, balancing quantitative and qualitative points to influence strategic decisions. 
  • You have experience working globally and are empathetic to customer needs
  • You have experience launching cross-functional go-to-market launches 
  • You have a track record of collaborating with a variety of cross-functional stakeholders in a fast-paced environment
  • You can explain the value of highly technical concepts in human, relatable, and compelling language. 
  • You have experience presenting to large industry groups and meeting with senior external stakeholders.

• We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! For this role, you can be within the North America region as long as we have a work location.
  • This team operates within the EST time zone for collaboration.

descriptionPlain:
We are seeking a Research Scientist to join our Artist-First AI Music lab. Our team pioneers and advances state-of-the-art generative technologies for music that create breakthrough experiences for fans and artists. We invent entirely new listening experiences that center and celebrate artists and creatives. All of our products will put artists and songwriters first, through these four principles:
 
Partnerships with record labels, distributors, and music publishers: We’ll develop new products for artists and fans through upfront agreements, not by asking for forgiveness later.
Choice in participation: We recognize there’s a wide range of views on use of generative music tools within the artistic community. Therefore, artists and rightsholders will choose if and how to participate to ensure the use of AI tools aligns with the values of the people behind the music.
Fair compensation and new revenue: We will build products that create wholly new revenue streams for rightsholders, artists, and songwriters, ensuring they are properly compensated for uses of their work and transparently credited for their contributions.
Artist-fan connection: AI tools we develop will not replace human artistry. They will give artists new ways to be creative and connect with fans. We will leverage our role as the place where more than 700 million people already come to listen to music every month to ensure that generative AI deepens artist-fan connections.
 
For more information, see this press release: https://newsroom.spotify.com/2025-10-16/artist-first-ai-music-spotify-collaboration/

lists.content:
• Conduct ground breaking research in music generation (diffusion, flow matching, or autoregressive models), as well as related domains like ML-based audio processing, music information retrieval, machine learning, and signal processing.
  • Run large-scale experiments with access to Spotify’s extensive infrastructure and an audience of more than 700 million monthly active users.
  • Create practical applications that harness generative technologies, pushing the boundaries of what’s possible in listening experiences.
  • Collaborate as part of a cross-functional team—working closely with scientists, engineers, product managers, designers, user researchers, and analysts—to craft innovative solutions to complex challenges.
  • Have a direct impact on Spotify’s products, tools, and services, working on projects that influence the entire organization.
  • Engage with the broader research community by publishing your findings, delivering talks, and attending top conferences.

• You have a Ph.D. degree in Computer Science, Mathematics, Engineering, or a related field. Previous industry experience is helpful.
  • You have experience in one or more of the following fields: generative modeling, machine learning, music information retrieval, speech processing, audio processing, signal processing, probabilistic modeling, computer vision, or related areas.
  • You have publications in communities such as ICASSP, ISMIR, INTERSPEECH, ICLR, AAAI, IJCAI, NeurIPS, ICML, CVPR, ECCV, ICCV, or related.
  • You have strong coding skills in the following languages/libraries: Python, PyTorch, NumPy.
  • You are a creative problem solver who is passionate about building outstanding products that add real value to millions of people.
  • You are enthusiastic about learning more about turning research ideas into products operating at scale.
  • You can explain complex topics in simple terms, and you enjoy building strong relationships with colleagues and stakeholders.

• We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! For this role, you can be within the North Americas region as long as we have a work location.
  • This team operates within the Eastern Standard time zone for collaboration.
  • Core working hours are CET 3pm-6pm / EST 9am-12pm.

descriptionPlain:
Spotify has more than 600M listeners in more than 180 markets around the world, who use our music, podcast, and audiobook services to find what delights, entertains, educates, and informs them. Personalization is a high impact organization that provides the technology to serve them what they expect to find, to help them explore and find new things to enjoy, and to suggest things they might not be aware of that they would like.


We are looking for a Research Scientist with a machine learning background to help us improve personalization experiences. You will join a team of researchers whose focus is on enhancing the Spotify experience by researching, developing, and integrating state-of-the-art generative AI technologies into our search and recommender systems, and unlock new interactive experiences.


You will be part of an interdisciplinary team focusing on ensuring that the foundations of Spotify technologies are at or above the state of the art and, in the process, redefine the state of the art for the field and contributing to the wider research community by publishing papers. Our team has strong ties internally to product groups as well as externally to the research community.


Areas of expertise: Information Retrieval, Recommendation System, Language Technologies, Foundational Models, Generative AI Technologies, and Machine Learning
description:
Spotify has more than 600M listeners in more than 180 markets around the world, who use our music, podcast, and audiobook services to find what delights, entertains, educates, and informs them. You will join a team of researchers whose focus is on enhancing the Spotify experience by researching, developing, and integrating state-of-the-art generative AI technologies into our search and recommender systems, and unlock new interactive experiences.


You will be part of an interdisciplinary team focusing on ensuring that the foundations of Spotify technologies are at or above the state of the art and, in the process, redefine the state of the art for the field and contributing to the wider research community by publishing papers. Our team has strong ties internally to product groups as well as externally to the research community.


Areas of expertise: Information Retrieval, Recommendation System, Language Technologies, Foundational Models, Generative AI Technologies, and Machine Learning

lists.content:
• Participate in groundbreaking research in artificial intelligence with a focus on large-scale generative applications to search and recommendation systems
  • Apply your scientific knowledge to analyze and collect data, identify problems, construct methodologies, including metrics and standard processes, conduct experiments, and devise scalable solutions through hands-on prototyping and development
  • Work on practical applications such as search, recommendation, language understanding in the music and talk audio domains
  • Collaborate with other scientists, engineers, product managers, designers, and data analysts across Spotify to design and develop innovative solutions to challenging problems
  • Impact Spotify products, while working on and iterating further a long-term research roadmap
  • Maintain a research profile through external engagement by publishing scientific papers, coordinating workshops, giving talks, and being an active community member at top conferences

• You have a PhD in either information retrieval, intelligent systems, machine learning, computer science, or related areas
  • You have expertise in domains such as language technologies, generative AI technologies, information retrieval, and recommender systems
  • You have peer-reviewed publications at journals or conferences such as RecSys, TheWebConf, WSDM, SIGIR, EMNLP, ACL, COLM, ICML, ICLR, NeurIPS, or related
  • You have solid hands-on skills in sourcing, cleaning, manipulating, analyzing, visualizing, and modeling of large scale real data
  • You are passionate about making sense of user behavior, music content, and talk audio content using best available methods
  • You are a creative problem-solver who is passionate about digging into complex problems and devising new approaches to reach results

• This role is based in London or Stockholm
  • We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! There will be some in person meetings, but still allows for flexibility to work from home

descriptionPlain:
Sell what you love. For us and millions of users across the globe, that’s Spotify. Join the Sales team and you’ll build the relationships that help grow our business in existing markets and beyond. We don’t just sell creative solutions to our clients and partners, we help to shape them; using our expert knowledge of ad products, sales channels and the industry to impact the way the world experiences music and podcasts. 


Our Ad Sales Training & Enablement team helps Spotify’s global Sales organization perform at its best. We design and deliver learning experiences that keep our sellers confident, informed, and customer-focused. Working closely with Sales Leadership, Product Marketing, and Business Marketing, we build programs that support growth across Spotify’s evolving advertising business.

Our Ad Sales Training & Enablement team helps Spotify’s global Sales organization perform at its best. We design and deliver learning experiences that keep our sellers confident, informed, and customer-focused. Working closely with Sales Leadership, Product Marketing, and Business Marketing, we build programs that support growth across Spotify’s evolving advertising business.

lists.content:
• Partner with sales leaders to shape and deliver training strategies for LATAM teams, with a focus on Mexico and Brazil
  • Lead engaging onboarding sessions for new hires, both virtually and in person
  • Collaborate with Product Marketing and Business Marketing to enable teams on new ad products and positioning
  • Design and facilitate interactive workshops that build core sales and account management skills
  • Assess learning needs and create clear, practical training materials that support day-to-day work
  • Provide coaching that helps sales team members grow their confidence and impact
  • Measure program effectiveness and use insights to continuously improve learning experiences

• You have 2+ years of experience in sales training, enablement, or a related field
  • You understand the digital media landscape, including programmatic advertising and/or social platforms
  • You know how to facilitate sessions that are engaging, inclusive, and tailored to different learning styles
  • You have experience building end-to-end training programs, from needs assessment to delivery
  • You build strong relationships with stakeholders across teams and levels
  • You communicate feedback clearly and supportively, helping others grow
  • You stay organized and manage multiple projects across regions with ease
  • You have experience in sales or media buying
  • You are fluent in Spanish and English, and you have working proficiency in Portuguese
  • Resume must be in English

• .This role is based in Sao Paulo
  • We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! There will be some in person meetings, but still allows for flexibility to work from home. We ask that you come in 2 times per week.

descriptionPlain:
Security engineers at Spotify protect the security of Spotify’s platform and of our 700+ million users. We are looking for an experienced engineer to join us in securing the most important engineering initiatives at Spotify.
 
You will be working in the product security engineering and consulting team. We’re a distributed team supporting autonomous development teams with application security expertise and best-in-class tooling. We aim to constantly improve the security posture for our fast-paced, rapidly-changing environment in a manner that will keep up with our scale. We’re experts in many domains of security, willing to teach and learn from anyone at the company.
 
You are a seasoned security, systems or software engineer with a passion for software security. Above all you have an insatiable appetite for learning new things and honing your existing skill set. In this role you are expected to represent security in various engineering and business contexts so we expect you to be comfortable communicating with diverse audiences both verbally and in writing.
description:
Security engineers at Spotify protect the security of Spotify’s platform and of our 700+ million users. We are looking for an experienced engineer to join us in securing the most important engineering initiatives at Spotify.
 
You will be working in the product security engineering and consulting team. We’re a distributed team supporting autonomous development teams with application security expertise and best-in-class tooling. We aim to constantly improve the security posture for our fast-paced, rapidly-changing environment in a manner that will keep up with our scale. We’re experts in many domains of security, willing to teach and learn from anyone at the company.
 
You are a seasoned security, systems or software engineer with a passion for software security. Above all you have an insatiable appetite for learning new things and honing your existing skill set. In this role you are expected to represent security in various engineering and business contexts so we expect you to be comfortable communicating with diverse audiences both verbally and in writing.

lists.content:
• Champion and contribute to the development and implementation of security best practices, standards, and automated tooling for secure development and deployment within Spotify's infrastructure, including AI-driven development.
  • Partner closely with teams across the company to integrate security seamlessly into their development lifecycle, from ideation to deployment and monitoring.
  • Consult, evangelize, and teach theoretical and practical security to groups of varying sizes, disciplines, and experience levels.
  • Drive cross-disciplinary initiatives to improve the security of our engineering ecosystem and the products developed at Spotify.
  • Conduct threat modeling, security reviews, and risk assessments for Spotify's diverse range of generative AI and non-AI systems.
  • Evaluate, prototype, and integrate specialized security tools for AI/ML systems.
  • Stay ahead of the curve on the rapidly evolving landscape of AI security threats, academic research, vulnerabilities, and mitigation strategies relevant to Spotify's scale and domain.
  • Contribute to security incident response activities involving AI systems.

• Hands-on technical experience with software security.
  • You are comfortable writing code to integrate security tools and automate your work with modern software development practices.
  • Security expertise in one or more domains, such as backend, mobile, web, and machine learning.
  • Strong foundation in core security domains such as cryptography, cloud security and application security.
  • You are comfortable working with diverse stakeholders and explaining security concepts to non-expert audiences.
  • You have experience from working in agile environments and easily adapt to change, enjoy challenges and thrive in ambiguity.
  • Ability to read and write code in languages such as Java, Python, Scala, C++ and TypeScript.
  • Experience with generative AI tools for common software engineering tasks.
  • Good understanding of common security risks, attack vectors, and vulnerabilities specific to AI/ML systems and how to mitigate them.
  • Demonstrable experience with security research on AI/ML systems and applications.
  • Experience integrating security tooling into production systems at scale.
  • Familiarity with common agentic AI frameworks.

• This role is based in either London or Stockholm
  • We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! There will be some in person meetings, but still allows for flexibility to work from home.

descriptionPlain:
The APAC Accounting Hub in Singapore plays a critical role in ensuring accurate financial reporting, strong compliance, and smooth coordination across markets. As part of Spotify’s global Finance team, we support a fast-growing, cross-border business that operates with speed, accountability, and high standards.
 
We’re looking for a highly self-motivated Senior Accountant to serve as the day-to-day Japan finance point of contact while supporting broader APAC reporting. This role is ideal for someone who is hands-on, structured, and comfortable operating in a lean, fast-paced environment with cross-border complexity. You’ll help embed Japanese language capability within the APAC team and partner with regional and global stakeholders across Finance and other functions as needed.
description:
The APAC Accounting Hub in Singapore plays a critical role in ensuring accurate financial reporting, strong compliance, and smooth coordination across markets. As part of Spotify’s global Finance team, we support a fast-growing, cross-border business that operates with speed, accountability, and high standards.
 
We’re looking for a highly self-motivated Senior Accountant to serve as the day-to-day Japan finance point of contact while supporting broader APAC reporting. This role is ideal for someone who is hands-on, structured, and comfortable operating in a lean, fast-paced environment with cross-border complexity. You’ll help embed Japanese language capability within the APAC team and partner with regional and global stakeholders across Finance and other functions as needed.

lists.content:
• Serve as the Japan finance point of contact within the APAC Accounting Hub, coordinating and escalating finance-related matters as needed
  • Own Japan reporting, statutory filings, accounting controls and compliance timelines
  • Partner with global Tax, Treasury, Legal, and APAC Accounting teams on Japan-related operational and compliance matters
  • Coordinate with Japan Business Process Outsourcing (BPO) provider on local execution processes, including WHT documentation, Japan tax office communications, Japan Citibank-related paper documentation, and physical submissions
  • Review and validate Japan statutory financial information and ensure alignment between IFRS and local GAAP reporting where required
  • Interpret feedback or notices from Japan tax authorities and external service providers, assess impact, and coordinate follow-up actions internally
  • Prepare and support consolidated APAC reporting during close cycles, statutory filings and related audit requirements for designated APAC entities as needed
  • Perform variance analysis and follow up on key issues identified across Japan and APAC reporting
  • Identify opportunities to streamline processes, reduce manual dependencies, and improve coordination efficiency
  • Participate in regional and global calls as required (night calls expected at not more than 5% of work time depending on close cycles and projects)
  • Contribute to special projects, system enhancements, and ad hoc analyses as assigned

• Minimum 5+ years related accounting operations experience within a multinational company, ideally in a technology or fast-paced environment
  • Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or related field
  • CPA, CA, or equivalent professional qualification preferred
  • Strong understanding of IFRS and exposure to local statutory reporting
  • Experience coordinating with external service providers such as audit firms, tax agents, BPO providers
  • Business-level Japanese (spoken and written) and strong professional English communication skills are required
  • Technically proficient with ERP systems and MS Office including advanced Excel skills
  • Strong analytical and communication skills across all levels
  • Comfortable managing cross-functional and external stakeholder coordination and navigating cross-cultural communication
  • Thrive in lean teams, take ownership of your work, and remain adaptable in evolving environments
• This role is based in Singapore and follows our hybrid work model, with an expectation of 2–3 days per week in the office

descriptionPlain: You’ll join the Music Publishing Deal Analysis team within FP&A, where we partner closely with Licensing, Legal, and Finance to shape the economics behind Spotify’s music business. This team plays a key role in evaluating and structuring publishing deals that support creators while ensuring long-term sustainability for Spotify. You’ll work at the intersection of music, data, and strategy, helping translate complex agreements into clear financial insights that guide decision-making.
description: You’ll join the Music Publishing Deal Analysis team within FP&A, where we partner closely with Licensing, Legal, and Finance to shape the economics behind Spotify’s music business. This team plays a key role in evaluating and structuring publishing deals that support creators while ensuring long-term sustainability for Spotify. You’ll work at the intersection of music, data, and strategy, helping translate complex agreements into clear financial insights that guide decision-making.

lists.content:
• Partner with Licensing, Accounting, and Legal teams to evaluate global and regional music publishing deals
  • Build and maintain financial models to assess deal economics, including scenario and sensitivity analysis
  • Translate contract terms into clear financial insights and materials to support decision-making
  • Track and develop KPIs to evaluate deal performance and long-term cost impact
  • Collaborate with Finance, Data, Engineering, and Product teams to support deal implementation and strategy
  • Communicate financial risks and opportunities to stakeholders and leadership in a clear, actionable way
  • Support ongoing deal operations, including amendments, renewals, and new rights categories
  • Improve internal tools, templates, and processes to streamline deal analysis
  • Stay informed on music industry trends, including publishing, independent labels, and regulatory changes

• You have 3+ years experience in FP&A, deal analysis, investment banking, consulting, or a similar analytical role
  • You are experienced in building financial models and working through structured analytical problems independently
  • You are comfortable working with large datasets and have experience with SQL or similar tools
  • You are interested in developing technical skills, including exposure to AI-enabled tools and workflows
  • You can interpret commercial terms and collaborate effectively with Legal, Business Affairs, or deal-focused teams
  • You balance strong attention to detail with sound judgment on priorities and materiality
  • You communicate clearly and can explain complex analysis to a range of stakeholders
  • You are curious, ask thoughtful questions, and enjoy building expertise in complex domains

• This role is based in New York, NY
  • We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! There will be some in person meetings, but still allows for flexibility to work from home. We ask that you come in 3 times per week

descriptionPlain:
Sell what you love. For us and millions of users across the globe, that’s Spotify. Join the Sales team and you’ll build the relationships that help grow our business in existing markets and beyond. We don’t just sell creative solutions to our clients and partners, we help to shape them; using our expert knowledge of ad products, sales channels and the industry to impact the way the world experiences music and podcasts. 


We are looking for a highly motivated individual to join our Business Operations team to join our Yield Strategy team in our APAC market. In this role, you will be responsible for using tools and systems to evaluate ad product and revenue performance across the entire Ad Sales business; act as a strategic partner across the ads business supporting Sales alongside other cross-functional teams, such as Business Operations, Finance, Sales Operations, and Product
description:
Sell what you love. For us and millions of users across the globe, that’s Spotify. Join the Sales team and you’ll build the relationships that help grow our business in existing markets and beyond. We don’t just sell creative solutions to our clients and partners, we help to shape them; using our expert knowledge of ad products, sales channels and the industry to impact the way the world experiences music and podcasts. 

We are looking for a highly motivated individual to join our Business Operations team to join our Yield Strategy team in our APAC market. In this role, you will be responsible for using tools and systems to evaluate ad product and revenue performance across the entire Ad Sales business; act as a strategic partner across the ads business supporting Sales alongside other cross-functional teams, such as Business Operations, Finance, Sales Operations, and Product

lists.content:
• Responsible for strategically managing pricing, inventory, and overall yield to maximise advertising revenue. This role involves serving as the individual point-person for developing and executing these strategies across the region
  • Partner with Yield Managers in other regions to share best practices to ensure local strategies fit within the larger Spotify global ad strategy and framework
  • Manipulate and analyse large data sets (dashboards, raw files) to inform data-led pricing and inventory decisions
  • Responsible for maintaining deep expertise in how APAC ad revenue is generated. Create systems, processes, and reporting to ensure critical inventory and revenue insights are quickly and clearly shared with internal stakeholders
  • Provide regular updates and actionable recommendations to APAC sales leadership to optimise yield while achieving revenue goals
  • Provide essential yield management support to APAC sales teams to ensure the region meets and exceeds revenue targets, aligning with Spotify's broader financial objectives
  • Collaborate cross-functionally with teams including Sales, Finance, Business Operations, and Sales Operations to drive ad revenue growth
  • Manage rate cards and deal approvals for the region in collaboration with Partnerships team

• You have a minimum of 5+ years of experience leading Media Planning, Pricing, and/or Digital Inventory and Yield for an Ad Agency, Publisher, Platform, Network or Ad Tech Vendor with a deep understanding of data analysis and data-driven decision-making.
  • Able to create and implement strategies that drive growth, optimising pricing and accurately reporting on business performance
  • Thorough understanding of the adsales pitch-to-pay process, specifically in the APAC region and in the Australia marketplace
  • Have an understanding of nuances of multiple markets in APAC region
  • Experience working with business intelligence/dashboarding tools such as Tableau, Google BigQuery, and/or other similar systems
  • Understanding of ad operations systems and the programmatic landscape, with demonstrated knowledge of Salesforce and various CRMs, OMSs, other advertising platforms (GAM, DV360, TTD, Magnite)
  • You understand the technical foundations of digital media, podcasts and programmatic mediums and proactively identify solutions to problems and predict/adapt to expect challenges before they happen
  • A highly organized and focused multi-tasker with strong attention to detail, analytical and project management skills, and can work independently within a team focused organization
  • You thrive in a fast-paced, deadline and detailed oriented environment

• This role is based in Sydney, Australia
  • We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! There will be some in person meetings, but still allows for flexibility to work from home.

descriptionPlain:
Mission Statement
The Platform team creates the technology that enables Spotify to learn quickly and scale easily, enabling rapid growth in our users and our business around the globe. Spanning many disciplines, we work to make the business work; creating the infrastructure, tooling, frameworks, and capabilities needed to welcome a billion customers.
About the Team
You’ll join the Data Platform within the Platform mission, where we build the foundations that help Spotify teams better understand and serve our listeners. Our work enables engineers and data scientists to explore user behavior at scale and make smarter, faster decisions.
As part of a collaborative and distributed engineering team, you’ll help shape the tools and SDKs that power data-driven product development across Spotify.
description:
Mission Statement

The Platform team creates the technology that enables Spotify to learn quickly and scale easily, enabling rapid growth in our users and our business around the globe. Spanning many disciplines, we work to make the business work; creating the infrastructure, tooling, frameworks, and capabilities needed to welcome a billion customers.

About the Team

You’ll join the Data Platform within the Platform mission, where we build the foundations that help Spotify teams better understand and serve our listeners. Our work enables engineers and data scientists to explore user behavior at scale and make smarter, faster decisions.

As part of a collaborative and distributed engineering team, you’ll help shape the tools and SDKs that power data-driven product development across Spotify.

lists.content:
• Build and evolve Android SDKs that enable engineers and data scientists to analyze user behavior at scale
  • Contribute to a high-performing, autonomous team of engineers, designers, and product managers
  • Develop scalable, testable, and maintainable code used across multiple teams and projects
  • Help define and improve Android engineering practices across Spotify
  • Collaborate closely with platform and data teams to deliver impactful solutions
  • Drive innovation and continuously improve developer productivity and experience
  • Support the broader Android community at Spotify and influence technical direction

• You are experienced in building Android applications and SDKs at scale
  • You have a deep understanding of modern Android development and tooling
  • You know how to write readable, maintainable, and performant code in Kotlin
  • You have some working experience with C++
  • You are comfortable supporting iOS if the need arises
  • You care about quality, testing practices, and performance
  • You are comfortable working in a collaborative, cross-functional environment
  • You have experience working with large-scale systems or data-heavy environments
  • You are curious, proactive, and eager to learn new technologies and domains
  • You are familiar with developer tooling, build systems, or data-related technologies

• This role is based in London
  • We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! There will be some in person meetings, but still allows for flexibility to work from home.

descriptionPlain:
We design Spotify’s consumer experience—end to end, moment to moment, across every screen, platform, and partner integration. Our mission is to make listening feel effortless, personal, and joyful for billions of users around the world. That means turning complexity into clarity across hundreds of touchpoints—from our mobile and desktop apps to the smart speakers, TVs, cars, and integrations where Spotify shows up every day. If it touches a consumer, we shape it. We bring deep insight into human behavior, design, and technology to craft experiences that feel intuitive, expressive, and unmistakably Spotify.
We are looking for a Senior Applied Research Engineer who brings deep research expertise and strong engineering skills across both audio and video. In this role, you will help advance how Spotify understands, evaluates, and improves media experiences across platforms. You’ll work across the full media pipeline—from backend processing such as ingestion, encoding, and delivery to frontend playback, buffering, and synchronization—solving product problems with research discipline: structured experiments, solid metrics, and evidence-based decisions, in a real production environment.
You’ll join a team working at the intersection of media quality, perception, and engineering. The team builds applied solutions that improve how audio and video experiences perform across Spotify. By turning deep technical understanding into scalable capabilities, they help ensure a consistent, high-quality experience for hundreds of millions of users across devices and platforms.
description:
We design Spotify’s consumer experience—end to end, moment to moment, across every screen, platform, and partner integration. Our mission is to make listening feel effortless, personal, and joyful for billions of users around the world. That means turning complexity into clarity across hundreds of touchpoints—from our mobile and desktop apps to the smart speakers, TVs, cars, and integrations where Spotify shows up every day. If it touches a consumer, we shape it. We bring deep insight into human behavior, design, and technology to craft experiences that feel intuitive, expressive, and unmistakably Spotify.

We are looking for a Senior Applied Research Engineer who brings deep research expertise and strong engineering skills across both audio and video. In this role, you will help advance how Spotify understands, evaluates, and improves media experiences across platforms. You’ll work across the full media pipeline—from backend processing such as ingestion, encoding, and delivery to frontend playback, buffering, and synchronization—solving product problems with research discipline: structured experiments, solid metrics, and evidence-based decisions, in a real production environment.

You’ll join a team working at the intersection of media quality, perception, and engineering. The team builds applied solutions that improve how audio and video experiences perform across Spotify. By turning deep technical understanding into scalable capabilities, they help ensure a consistent, high-quality experience for hundreds of millions of users across devices and platforms.

lists.content:
• Own research-to-production work across audio/video signal processing, perceptual quality, and media intelligence.

  • Partner with teams to integrate prototypes into production systems and decision-making across areas like music and video information retrieval and quality-aware processing.

  • Work across the full media pipeline, from encoding and delivery to playback, buffering, and synchronization.

  • Build evaluation frameworks and support media platform and codec decisions grounded in measurement.

  • Design perceptual studies, develop tooling that connects quality metrics to real user experience outcomes.

  • Collaborate with multi-functional teams, turning research insights into scalable capabilities used across Spotify.

  • Help improve how we evaluate and enhance media quality across the platform.

  • Drive high-impact initiatives from idea through to measurable outcomes.

• You have 8+ years of experience in applied research and engineering in media technology, audio, or video.

  • You bring deep expertise in audio and/or video, with working knowledge across both domains.

  • You understand media systems end-to-end, from backend processing through to client-side playback.

  • You have strong foundations in signal processing and experience with perceptual quality or feature design.

  • You are comfortable working in Python and have experience with C++ or similar languages.

  • You have experience running perceptual evaluations or quality benchmarking.

  • You enjoy building systems that move from research into real-world impact.

  • You collaborate effectively across disciplines and communicate complex ideas clearly.

  • You are curious, pragmatic, and motivated by solving meaningful problems.

• This role is based in London or Stockholm.
  • We offer you the  flexibility to work where you work best! There will be some in person meetings, but still allows for flexibility to work from home.
